          <ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. StV-2 Capability Taxonomy" />
          <ac:plain-text-link-body>
           StV-2 Capability Taxonomy
          </ac:plain-text-link-body>
         </ac:link></td>
        <td><p>Capabilities shown in StV-5 table are usually modeled in StV-2 Capability Taxonomy.</p></td>
       </tr>
       <tr>
        <td>
         <div class="content-wrapper">
          <p>
           <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="46">
            <ri:attachment ri:filename="Acv2_icon.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping" /></ri:attachment>
           </ac:image></p>
         </div></td>
        <td>
         <ac:link>
          <ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. AcV-2 Programme Timelines" />
          <ac:plain-text-link-body>
           AcV-2 Programme Timelines
          </ac:plain-text-link-body>
         </ac:link></td>
        <td><p>The timing is based on project milestones from AcV2 which indicate when capability configuration will be<br />delivered to an organiation.</p></td>
       </tr>
       <tr>
        <td>
         <div class="content-wrapper">
          <p>
           <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="44">
            <ri:attachment ri:filename="Ov4_icon.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping" /></ri:attachment>
           </ac:image></p>
         </div></td>
        <td><p>
          <ac:link>
           <ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" />
           <ac:plain-text-link-body>
            OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart
           </ac:plain-text-link-body>
          </ac:link></p></td>
        <td><p>Actual Organizational Resources related to deployment of Capability Configurations are taken from OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart.</p></td>
       </tr>
      </tbody>
     </table>
     <p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
    </ac:layout-cell>
    <ac:layout-cell>
     <p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

      </ac:parameter>
      <ac:rich-text-body>
       <ul>
        <li>Capability</li>
        <li>Capability Configuration</li>
        <li>Exhibits</li>
        <li>Actual Organization</li>
        <li>Actual Post</li>
        <li>Deployed Milestone</li>
        <li>No Longer Used Milestone</li>
        <li>Actual Project</li>
        <li>Enterprise Phase</li>
        <li>Whole-Life Enterprise</li>
       </ul>
      </ac:rich-text-body>
     </ac:structured-macro>
     <p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
     <ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b6b4448-cb3d-441f-9776-86d417dcc2d8">

       Related procedures
      </ac:parameter>

       <ul>
        <li>Creating StV-5 table</li>
        <li>Modifying StV-5 table</li>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990195 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00347: MODAF. StV-6 Operational Activity to Capability Mapping

- page_id: `153990195`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990195/MODAF.+StV-6+Operational+Activity+to+Capability+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:14.531+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The StV-6 describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the operational activities that those capabilities support.

**Implementation**

StV-6 can be represented using a StV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities will be used as row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StV-6 Operational Activity to Capability Mapping

**Related views**

The StV-6 View provides a bridge between capability analyses using StVs and operational activities analyzed using OVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the SV-5 which maps system functions to operational activities.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Standard Operational Activity
- Maps to Capability

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building StV-6 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The Capabilities will be used as row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as column elements.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Stv_6.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. It is similar in function to the SV-5 which maps system functions to operational activities.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9539cd55-012a-4ebc-a485-6c717edc6876"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Standard Operational Activity</li><li>Maps to Capability</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95697d58-78a8-4dfa-925a-147e92cef80f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Building StV-6 matrix</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /><br /><br /><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990241 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00348: MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification

- page_id: `153990241`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990241/MODAF.+SV-1+Resource+Interaction+Specification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.128+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Resource Interaction Specification (SV-1) addresses the composition and interaction of resources. SV-1 now incorporates the human elements – Posts, Organizations and Roles. This view was previously known as the System Interface Description; the name change reflects the expanded scope of modeling in the solution space. The Resource Interaction Specification (SV-1) links together the operational and systems architecture views by depicting how resources are structured and interact in order to realize the logical architecture specified in an OV-2. An SV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an OV-2 (i.e. in a to-be architecture), and so there may be many alternative SV configurations that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an as-is architecture, the OV-2 may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SV-1 to allow communication of key information flows to non-technical stakeholders. A resource interaction is a simplified representation of a pathway or network, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e. a line with possible amplifying information). The SV-1 depicts all interactions between resources that are of interest to the architect.

Note that interactions between systems may be further specified in detail in the SV-2 and SV-6. Sub-resource assemblies may be identified in the SV-1 to any level (i.e. depth) of decomposition the architect sees fit. The SV-1 may also identify the Physical Assets (e.g. Platforms) at which resources are deployed, and optionally overlay Operational Nodes that utilize those resources. In many cases, an operational node depicted in an OV-2 product may well be the logical representation of the resource that is shown in the SV-1.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification

**Related views**

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with nodes originally specified in an OV-2. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one resource to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an SV-2. Resource Interactions are summarized in a Resource Interactions Matrix (SV-3).

The functions performed by the resources are specified in an SV-4 Resource Functionality Description, but may optionally be overlaid on the Resources in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As OV-2 and OV-5 specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and SV-4 specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Resource Port
- Resource Connector
- Capability
- Exhibits
- Fielded Capability
- Service
- Request
- Resource Interaction
- Control
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-1 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-1 diagram

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Resource Interaction Creation Wizard
- Resource Interaction Manager Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

SV-1 now incorporates the human elements – Posts, Organizations and Roles. This view was previously known as the System Interface Description; the name change reflects the expanded scope of modeling in the solution space. The Resource Interaction Specification (SV-1) links together the operational and systems architecture views by depicting how resources are structured and interact in order to realize the logical architecture specified in an OV-2. An SV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an OV-2 (i.e. in a to-be architecture), and so there may be many alternative SV configurations that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an as-is architecture, the OV-2 may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SV-1 to allow communication of key information flows to non-technical stakeholders. A resource interaction is a simplified representation of a pathway or network, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e. a line with possible amplifying information). The SV-1 depicts all interactions between resources that are of interest to the architect. </p><p>Note that interactions between systems may be further specified in detail in the SV-2 and SV-6. Sub-resource assemblies may be identified in the SV-1 to any level (i.e. depth) of decomposition the architect sees fit. The SV-1 may also identify the Physical Assets (e.g. Platforms) at which resources are deployed, and optionally overlay Operational Nodes that utilize those resources. In many cases, an operational node depicted in an OV-2 product may well be the logical representation of the resource that is shown in the SV-1.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>A UML Class diagram.</li><li>A UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>A SysML Block Definition diagram.</li><li>A SysML Internal Block diagram.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_1.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with nodes originally specified in an OV-2. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.</p><p>An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one resource to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an SV-2. Resource Interactions are summarized in a Resource Interactions Matrix (SV-3).</p><p>The functions performed by the resources are specified in an SV-4 Resource Functionality Description, but may optionally be overlaid on the Resources in the SV-1.</p><p>An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As OV-2 and OV-5 specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and SV-4 specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e83c577-e5b8-43b6-b6d6-0003cf416836"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Artifact</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization</li><li>Post</li><li>Resource Role</li><li>Resource Interface</li><li>Resource Port</li><li>Resource Connector</li><li>Capability</li><li>Exhibits</li><li>Fielded Capability</li><li>Service</li><li>Request</li><li>Resource Interaction</li><li>Control</li><li>Exchange Element</li><li>Energy</li><li>Materiel</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="61f677a6-546d-413f-8b00-a3db92770ac9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating SV-1 diagram</li><li>Creating Resource Interaction in SV-1 diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e56a9292-c5dc-48f9-a890-5d8bfa5b1c27"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related references</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Interaction Creation Wizard</li><li>Resource Interaction Manager Dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990267 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00350: MODAF. SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification

- page_id: `153990267`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990267/MODAF.+SV-10a+Resource+Constraints+Specification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.583+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose the SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification is to specify functional and non-functional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (i.e. the structural and behavioral elements of the SV viewpoint). SV-10a describes constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the SV physical 
architecture.

The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e. non-functional).

**Implementation**

SV-10a can be represented using:

- A SV-10a table.
- A SV-10a spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification

**Related views**

SV-10a describes constraints on resources, functions, and data that make up the SV physical architecture.

Where a Resource Constraint is based on some standard, then that standard should be listed in the Standards Profile (TV-1).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Constraint
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Function
- Entity Item
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-10a table

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990269 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00351: MODAF. SV-10b Resource State Transition Description

- page_id: `153990269`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990269/MODAF.+SV-10b+Resource+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.618+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Resource State Transition Description (SV-10b) is a graphical method of describing a ResourceType’s (or Function’s) response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the subjects will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

**Implementation**

SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-10b Resource State Transition Description

**Related views**

An SV-10a describes constraints on resources, functions, and data that make up the SV physical architecture.

Where a Resource Constraint is based on some standard, then that standard should be listed in the Standards Profile (TV-1).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resources State Machine
- Resource State
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Resource State Transition Description (SV-10b) is a graphical method of describing a ResourceType’s (or Function’s) response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the subjects will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_10b.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-10b Resource State Transition Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>SV-10b Resource State Transition Description</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>An SV-10a describes constraints on resources, functions, and data that make up the SV physical architecture.</p><p>Where a Resource Constraint is based on some standard, then that standard should be listed in the Standards Profile (TV-1).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dbbcb180-0fe3-43dd-b8a4-76244737dc8e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resources State Machine</li><li>Resource State</li><li>Resource Artifact</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization</li><li>Post</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990271 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00352: MODAF. SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `153990271`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990271/MODAF.+SV-10c+Resource+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.654+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Resource Event-Trace Description provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between functional resources. Each event-trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation. The SV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating Resource or System Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, in order to perform its assigned functionality.

**Implementation**

SV-10c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description

You can also create SV-10c Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin. For more information on how to model the business process diagram, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='BPMN Process Diagram' space='CBMP2021x'].

**Related views**

The SV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the Resource State Transition Description (SV-10b) to describe the dynamic behavior of resources.

The data content of ‘messages’ that connect life-lines in an SV-10c View Product may be related, in modelling terms, with resource interactions (SV-1, 3), data flows (SV-4, 6) and data schema entities (SV-11) modeled in other views.

- Resource Event Trace
- Resource Message
- Resource Role
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post

- Resource Interaction Creation Wizard
- Resource Interaction Manager Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990273 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00353: MODAF. SV-11 Physical Schema

- page_id: `153990273`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990273/MODAF.+SV-11+Physical+Schema
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.691+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-11 View defines the structure of the various kinds of system data that are utilized by the systems in the Architecture. The Physical Schema is one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. SV-11 is used to describe how the information represented in the Information Model (OV-7) is actually implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g. entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.

****

**Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration**

You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for conceptual, logical, and physical data modeling in SV-11. It supports the information engineering notation within this view.

Note that the Entity Relationship diagram is supported in SV-11. It allows for using the information engineering notation within this view.

**Implementation**

SV-11 can be represented using:

- A SV-11 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-11 Physical Schema

**Related views**

The Physical Schema is one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. An SV-11 is used to describe how information represented in the Information Model (OV-7) is actually implemented.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Entity Item
- Entity Attribute
- Entity Relationship
- Exchange Element
- Details
- Physical Data Model

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990275 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00354: MODAF. SV-12 Service Provision

- page_id: `153990275`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990275/MODAF.+SV-12+Service+Provision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.729+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-12 defines the relationships between the Capability Configurations and Services.

**Implementation**

SV-12 can be represented using a SV-12 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Service Interfaces will be used as the row elements and the Resources will be used as the column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-12 Service Provision

**Related views**

The service attributes defined in SOV-1 can be given values in an SV-12 and related to the environment under which those values are true.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Service Interface
- Service
- Request

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building SV-12 Service Provision matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990245 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00355: MODAF. SV-2 Resource Communications Description

- page_id: `153990245`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990245/MODAF.+SV-2+Resource+Communications+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.192+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Systems Communications Description series of views specifies the communications networks and pathways 
that link systems, and provides details regarding their configuration. The networks and pathways documented 
through these views represent the physical implementation of the information needlines identified in an 
Operational Node Connectivity Description (OV-2). The SV-2 series focuses on the physical characteristics of 
each link, to include specification of such attributes as the geographic location of network components (e.g. 
routers, switches, amplifiers and repeaters). Attributes such as capacities (e.g. bandwidth, throughput), 
frequencies used, security encryption methods used, and other descriptive information are usually presented in a 
corresponding SV-6 product (though most architecture tools would prompt the architect to enter such data as the 
SV-2 views are being developed).

**Implementation**

SV-2 can be represented using:

- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

**Related views**

Any protocol and Standard referred to in an SV-2 diagram must be defined in the TV-1 Technical Standards View.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Resource Port
- Resource Connector
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Protocol
- Standard
- Service
- Request
- Resource Interaction
- Control
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-2 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Resource Interaction Creation Wizard
- Resource Interaction Manager Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The networks and pathways documented<br />through these views represent the physical implementation of the information needlines identified in an<br />Operational Node Connectivity Description (OV-2). The SV-2 series focuses on the physical characteristics of<br />each link, to include specification of such attributes as the geographic location of network components (e.g.<br />routers, switches, amplifiers and repeaters). SV-6 product (though most architecture tools would prompt the architect to enter such data as the<br />SV-2 views are being developed).</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-2 can be Standard referred to in an SV-2 diagram must be defined in the TV-1 Technical Standards View.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990248 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00357: MODAF. SV-3 Resource Interaction Matrix

- page_id: `153990248`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990248/MODAF.+SV-3+Resource+Interaction+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.240+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Resource Interaction Matrix provides a tabular summary of the resource interactions specified in the SV-1 for the Architecture. An SV-3 Product allows a quick overview of all the resource interactions specified in one or more SV-1 diagrams. The SV-3 can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of groups of system pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose.

**Implementation**

SV-3 can be represented using a SV-3 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. Rows and Columns in the matrix represent the Resources.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-3 Resource Interaction Matrix

**Related views**

SV-3 is a summary description of the resource interactions that are identified in SV-1.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Exchange Element
- Resource Interaction
- Materiel
- Energy

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building SV-3 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00358: MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description

- page_id: `153990250`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990250/MODAF.+SV-4+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.271+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Functionality Descriptions (SV-4) address human and system functionality. The primary purposes of SV-4 are 
to develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by 
each resource, ensure that the functional connectivity is complete, and ensure that the functional decomposition 
reaches an appropriate level of detail. The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the 
allocation of functions to resources and flow of data between functions. The SV-4 is the systems view counterpart.

**Implementation**

SV-4 can be represented using:

- A SV-4 diagram for Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.
- An SV-4 diagram for Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of SV-4 Functionality Description Flow

You can also create SV-4 Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin. For more information on how to model the business process diagram, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='BPMN Process Diagram' space='CBMP2021x'].

**Related views**

An SV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the SV-1 (in the same way that OV-5 is the behavioral counterpart to OV2).

The functions are likely to be related to Operational Activities captured in an OV-5. Although there is a correlation between the Operational Activity Model (OV-5) and the functional hierarchy of SV-4, it need not be a one-to-one mapping, hence, the need for a Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5), which provides that mapping.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Function
- Operational Activity
- Standard Operational Activity
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Is Capable Of Performing
- Function Action
- Function Edge
- Function Parameter
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-4 Functionality Description diagram
- Creating SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Resource Interaction Creation Wizard
- Resource Interaction Manager Dialog

The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the<br />allocation of functions to resources and flow of data between functions. The SV-4 is the systems view counterpart.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-4 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-4 diagram for Function hierarchies. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990252 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00359: MODAF. SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `153990252`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990252/MODAF.+SV-5+Function+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.309+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5) addresses the linkage between functions described in an SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in an OV-5. The SV-5 View depicts the mapping of functions (and, optionally, the functional resources that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.

**Implementation**

SV-5 can be represented using a SV-5 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Functions will be used as the row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as the column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

**Related views**

An SV-5 addresses the linkage between the functions described in an SV-4 and the Operational Activities specified in an OV-5.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Function
- Operational Activity
- Implements

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building SV-5 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990254 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00360: MODAF. SV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix

- page_id: `153990254`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990254/MODAF.+SV-6+Systems+Data+Exchange+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.349+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Systems Data Exchange Matrix specifies the characteristics of the system data exchanged between systems. The focus is on data crossing the system boundary. An SV-6 focuses on the specific aspects of the system data flow and the system data content in a tabular format.

**Implementation**

SV-6 can be represented using:

- A SV-6 table.
- An SV-6 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Resource Roles(resource usages).
- A SV-6 spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Systems Data Exchange Matrix

**Related views**

SV-6 is the physical equivalent of the logical OV-3 table and provides detailed information on the system connections which implement the information exchanges specified in an OV-3.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Exchange Element
- Resource Interaction
- Materiel
- Energy

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Adding Existing Resource Interaction to SV-6 table

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

An SV-6 focuses on the specific aspects of the system data flow and the system data content in a tabular format.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-6 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-6 table.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">An SV-6 role-based table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b11eb514-520f-4cf6-84e4-90140cad3034"><ac:rich-text-body>A role-based table represents exchanges between Resource Roles(resource usages).</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>A SV-6 spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_6.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990256 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00361: MODAF. SV-7 Resource Performance Parameters Matrix

- page_id: `153990256`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990256/MODAF.+SV-7+Resource+Performance+Parameters+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.392+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-7 is the Resource Performance Parameters Matrix and depicts the performance characteristics of a Functional Resource (system, role, or capability configuration). The Resource Performance Parameters Matrix expands on the information presented in an SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the Functional Resources shown in the SV-1. The Resource Performance Parameters Matrix View specifies qualitative and quantitative characteristics of functional resources and the performance parameters of each resource. The performance parameters are selected by the architect and end user community.

**Implementation**

SV-7 can be represented using:

- A SV-7 typical parameters table.
- A SV-7 actual parameters table.
- A SV-7 actual parameters spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-7 Typical table

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-7 Actual table

**Related views**

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification | The Resource Performance Parameters Matrix expands on the information presented in an SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the resources shown in the SV-1. |
|  | SV-2 Resource Communications Description | SV-7 expands on the information presented in an SV-2 by depicting the characteristics of the Resources shown in the SV-2. |

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Artifact
- Software
- System
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Measurement Set
- Actual Property Set
- Measurement
- Actual Measurement

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-7 Typical table
- Creating SV-7 Actual table
- Generating SV-7 Actual table from SV-7 Typical table
- Generating reports

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990261 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00362: MODAF. SV-8 Capability Configuration Management

- page_id: `153990261`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990261/MODAF.+SV-8+Capability+Configuration+Management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.479+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Capability Configuration Management view presents a whole lifecycle view of a resource, describing how its configuration changes over time. The SV-8 provides an overview of how a capability configuration structure changes over time. It shows the structure of several capability configurations mapped against a timeline.

**Implementation**

SV-8 can be represented using a SV-8 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-8 Capability Configuration Management

**Related views**

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |
|  |  | The changes depicted in the SV-8 View are derived from the project milestones that are shown using an AcV-2. When the AcV-2 is used to model project timelines for capability acquisition projects, there is likely to be a close relationship between these two Views. |

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Whole Life Configuration
- Version Of Configuration
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990265 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00363: MODAF. SV-9 Technology & Skills Forecast

- page_id: `153990265`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990265/MODAF.+SV-9+Technology+Skills+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.548+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Technology & Skills Forecast defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements/ trends. New technologies and skills will be tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SV-8 milestones and linked to Enterprise Phases. SV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the Resources that constitute the Architecture. The SV-9 provides descriptions of relevant: emerging capabilities, industry trends, predictions of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software products, and current and possible future skills In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and products, the SV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture.

**Implementation**

SV-9 can be represented using a SV-9 table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-9 Technology & Skills Forecast

**Related views**

The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) will be coordinated with the Architecture transition plans (see SV-8). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading / re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.

If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given Architecture, then it may be convenient to combine the SV-9 with the Technical Standards Forecast (TV-2).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Forecast
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Protocol
- Standard

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating SV-9 table

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Time Periods Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

New technologies and skills will be tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SV-8 milestones and linked to Enterprise Phases. SV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the Resources that constitute the Architecture. The SV-9 provides descriptions of relevant: emerging capabilities, industry trends, predictions of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software products, and current and possible future skills In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and products, the SV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-9 can be represented using a SV-9 table.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_9.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-9 Technology &amp; Skills Forecast" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>SV-9 Technology &amp; Skills Forecast</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) will be coordinated with the Architecture transition plans (see SV-8). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading / re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.</p><p>If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given Architecture, then it may be convenient to combine the SV-9 with the Technical Standards Forecast (TV-2).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85d87550-b43a-4e04-9ef1-234160dcca9a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Forecast</li><li>Resource Artifact</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization</li><li>Post</li><li>Protocol</li><li>Standard</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d161b2a1-4a94-492b-9d2a-f4a492a32d0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating SV-9 table</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b975fd5-5418-466d-88b6-895f2747c97f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related references</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Time Periods Dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990278 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00364: MODAF. TV-1 Standards Profile

- page_id: `153990278`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990278/MODAF.+TV-1+Standards+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.783+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A TV-1 defines the technical and non-technical standards, guidance, and policy applicable to the architecture.

As well as identifying applicable technical standards, the TV-1 may document the policies and standards that apply to the operational or business context.

**Implementation**

TV-1 can be represented by a TV-1 table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### TV-1 Standards Profile

**Related views**

A TV-1 serves as the bridge between the SV and TV. The SV-9 forecasts relate to the TV-1 in that a timed technology forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a system element.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Standard
- Protocol

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating TV-1 table

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990280 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00365: MODAF. TV-2 Standards Forecast

- page_id: `153990280`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990280/MODAF.+TV-2+Standards+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:07:15.820+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > MODAF > MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards needs to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the SV-8 and SV-9 views.

A Standards Forecast is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems and business processes covered by the architecture. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given architecture description is being built, and should identify issues that will affect the architecture.

A TV-2 complements and expands on the Standards Profile (TV-1) product and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture. For standards advice refer to the JSP 602 series of documents.

One of the prime purposes of this Product is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the Architecture and its constituent elements.

**Implementation**

TV-2 can be represented using a TV-2 table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### TV-2 Standards Forecast

**Related views**

A TV-2 delineates the standards that will potentially impact the relevant system elements (from SV-1, SV-2, SV-4, SV-6, and OV-7) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the SV-8 and SV-9. A system’s evolution, specified in the SV-8, may be tied to a future standard listed in the TV-2. A timed technology forecast from the SV9 is related to a TV-2 standards forecast in the following manner: a certain technology may be dependent on a TV2 standard (for example, a standard listed in TV-2 may not be adopted until a certain technology becomes available).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Standard
- Protocol
- Forecast

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating TV-2 table

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Time Periods Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards needs to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the SV-8 and SV-9 views.</p><p>A Standards Forecast is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems and business processes covered by the architecture. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given architecture description is being built, and should identify issues that will affect the architecture.</p><p>A TV-2 complements and expands on the Standards Profile (TV-1) product and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture. For standards advice refer to the JSP 602 series of documents.</p><p>One of the prime purposes of this Product is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the Architecture and its constituent elements.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>TV-2 can be represented using a TV-2 table.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tv_2.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. TV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>TV-2 Standards Forecast</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>A TV-2 delineates the standards that will potentially impact the relevant system elements (from SV-1, SV-2, SV-4, SV-6, and OV-7) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the SV-8 and SV-9. A system’s evolution, specified in the SV-8, may be tied to a future standard listed in the TV-2. A timed technology forecast from the SV9 is related to a TV-2 standards forecast in the following manner: a certain technology may be dependent on a TV2 standard (for example, a standard listed in TV-2 may not be adopted until a certain technology becomes available).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="588d4794-1e31-43c5-bfa9-a4bcd20e04f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Standard</li><li>Protocol</li><li>Forecast</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5abc4ba6-7a82-4270-be6a-7903c9b6afb0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating TV-2 table</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d6c355d-ada1-4642-b4cf-f7531d8372fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related references</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Time Periods Dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990914 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00366: Modifying criteria through the legend

- page_id: `153990914`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990914/Modifying+criteria+through+the+legend
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-27T10:54:32.348+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Traceability and Impact Analysis > Traceability relation maps
- labels: ['criteria-color', 'select-criteria', 'modify-criteria']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying criteria through the legend' space='UPDM2P2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying criteria through the legend' space='UPDM2P2024x'] through the map legend.

colorTo change the criteria color

1. Double-click the Legend. The Relation Criteria dialog opens.
2. Select the criteria for which you want to change the color.
3. In the Color list, select the desired color.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

otherTo select other criteria

1. Double-click the Legend.
2. In the Relation Criteria dialog choose the Simple Navigation property group.
3. Click to clear the Show Relations Criteria Available Only for Context check-box. The list of available criteria is displayed.
4. Select the preferred criteria.
5. Click OK , when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can <ac:link ac:anchor="color"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[change the colors of the criteria relationships]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Modifying criteria through the legend" /></ac:link> and <ac:link ac:anchor="other"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[select other criteria]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Modifying criteria through the legend" /></ac:link> through the map legend.</p><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor"><ac:parameter ac:name="">color</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To change the criteria color</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the Legend. The <strong>Relation Criteria</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the criteria for which you want to change the color.</li><li>In the <strong>Color</strong> list, select the desired color.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor"><ac:parameter ac:name="">other</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To select other criteria</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the Legend.</li><li>In the <strong>Relation Criteria</strong> dialog choose the <strong>Simple Navigation</strong> property group.</li><li>Click to clear the <strong>Show Relations Criteria Available Only for Context</strong> check-box. The list of available criteria is displayed.</li><li>Select the preferred criteria.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153989974 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00367: Modifying CV-5 table

- page_id: `153989974`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153989974/Modifying+CV-5+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:34:32.857+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Capability viewpoint > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping
- labels: ['remove-resources-from-cv-5', 'remove-capabilities-from-cv-5', 'modify-cv-5', 'add-capabilities-in-cv-5', 'add-resources-in-cv-5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1415794279

1415794281

1415794280

**To add/ remove Capabilities in/from a CV-5 table**

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to add or remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.A Capability of Performer relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.A Capability of Performer relationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.

**To add/ remove Resources in/from a CV-5 table**

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. The + and - buttons appears at the right of the cell. [ATTACHMENT filename='Dr_table_remove_resources.png']
3. Do one of the following:
  - To add a Resource, select a Resource and click the + button . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment Milestones wizard' space='UPDM2P2024x']**Deployment Milestones** wizard opens.
  - To remove a Resource, select a Resource and click the - button.

For more information about table management, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Tables' space='UPDM2P2024x'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990553 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00368: Modifying Dr table

- page_id: `153990553`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990553/Modifying+Dr+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T10:07:54.651+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Deployed Resources viewpoint > Dr Deployment Schedule
- labels: ['add-capabilities-in-dr', 'remove-capabilities-from-dr', 'remove-resources-from-dr', 'remove-actual-organizational-resources-from-dr', 'remove-actual-organizational-resources-from-model', 'manage-dr-table', 'modify-dr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add/remove Capabilities in/from Dr table

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to add or to remove them from the table.
3. Click OK when you are done.

- The Capabilities are removed only from the table but not from the model.
- An Exhibits relationship between the context element and Capability is added/removed from the model.

To remove Resources from Dr table

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource you want to remove and click the - button. [ATTACHMENT filename='Dr_table_remove_resources.png']

The Resource is removed only from the table but not from the model.

To remove Actual Organizational Resources from Dr table

1. Select Actual Organizational Resource.
2. Click Remove from Table button in table toolbar.

To remove Actual Organizational Resources from model

1. Select Actual Organizational Resource.
2. Click Delete button in table toolbar.

For more information about table management, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Tables' space='UPDM2P2024x'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990282 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00369: NAF

- page_id: `153990282`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All NAF views are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990387 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00370: NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views

- page_id: `153990387`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990387/NAF+4.0+viewpoints+and+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-17T11:16:56.533+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: ['naf-4', 'nato-architecture-framework']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NATO Architecture Framework (NAF) is a standard for developing Enterprise Architectures. An Enterprise Architecture is a model of a current or future state of an enterprise. An enterprise could be an organization, a system (including the human factors) or a project. The purpose of enterprise architecture is to capture the complex dependencies that exist in large-scale systems of systems so as to aid with decision support. NAF provides a standard way to model the architecture.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

800600

#### PANEL: NAF 4.0 viewpoints

NAF 4.0 viewpoints

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

An Enterprise Architecture is a model of a current or future state of an enterprise. An enterprise could be an organization, a <span class="wp-glossary wpg-tooltip">system</span> (including the human factors) or a project. The purpose of enterprise architecture is to capture the complex dependencies that exist in large-scale systems of systems so as to aid with decision support. NAF provides a standard way to model the architecture.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="NAF 4.0 Viewpoints and Views" ac:title="NAF 4.0 Viewpoints and Views"><ri:attachment ri:filename="viewpoints-views_naf4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="a8f2f393-ea58-4796-9db9-ff07eddbf5c6" ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">800</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9amaJGkw9xk" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">600</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="71e38be2-9fd3-421a-82b6-e94766f454bd" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">NAF 4.0 viewpoints</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="f75fe6f2-78a5-4558-a8bd-70f83ce7344e" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990283 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00371: NATO All View Viewpoint

- page_id: `153990283`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990283/NATO+All+View+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.625+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are some overarching aspects of architectures that relate to all views. These overarching aspects are captured in the NATO All View (NAV). The NAV products provide information pertinent to the entire architecture but do not represent a distinct view of the architecture. NAV subviews set the scope and context of the architecture. The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>There are some overarching aspects of architectures that relate to all views. These overarching aspects are captured in the NATO All View (NAV). The NAV products provide information pertinent to the entire architecture but do not represent a distinct view of the architecture. NAV subviews set the scope and context of the architecture. The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e4daac0e-c219-49ec-a536-ac0d091ebc35" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990289 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00372: NATO Capability Viewpoint

- page_id: `153990289`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990289/NATO+Capability+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.690+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NATO Capability View (NCV) supports the process of analyzing and optimizing the delivery of military capabilities in line with NATO‘s strategic intent. The NCV achieves this by capturing essential elements of NATO‘s strategic vision and concepts and NATO‘s capability planning process, and decomposing this data into a capability taxonomy. The taxonomy is augmented with schedule data and measures of effectiveness to enable the analysis of capability gaps and overlaps. The NCV further details the dependencies between military capabilities, enabling 
capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NATO Capability View (NCV) supports the process of analyzing and optimizing the delivery of military capabilities in line with NATO‘s strategic intent. The NCV achieves this by capturing essential elements of NATO‘s strategic vision and concepts and NATO‘s capability planning process, and decomposing this data into a capability taxonomy. The taxonomy is augmented with schedule data and measures of effectiveness to enable the analysis of capability gaps and overlaps. The NCV further details the dependencies between military capabilities, enabling<br />capability options to be built in a more coherent manner and effective trade-offs to be conducted across NATO common funded programmes.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdbfe801-97db-4028-b95f-fb30b8478177"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xD91EYVU-bM" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>  <ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c662a95d-1b50-48a6-bd99-a750714426b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LB1Ure3ravQ" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br /></p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="eaffc0e1-cfb5-4837-bc3e-4b9daf59bad7" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990314 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00373: NATO Operational Viewpoint

- page_id: `153990314`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990314/NATO+Operational+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.960+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NOV is a description of the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to accomplish NATO missions. The NOV contains graphical and textual products that comprise an identification of the nodes, their assigned tasks and activities, and dependencies between nodes. It defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NOV is a description of the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to accomplish NATO missions. The NOV contains graphical and textual products that comprise an identification of the nodes, their assigned tasks and activities, and dependencies between nodes. It defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99b8c7c0-49af-4ecc-929f-17777e8e052a"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jrpPuZ8Vf74" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f9c5597d-1c9b-46ac-b11b-bb539523bda6" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990308 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00374: NATO Programme Viewpoint

- page_id: `153990308`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990308/NATO+Programme+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.894+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Programme views are introduced in the NAF Version 3 to describe the relationships between NATO capability requirements and the various programmes and projects being implemented. They provide programmatic details 
and highlight the dependencies between capability management and the NATO acquisition process. This information can be further leveraged to show the impact of acquisition decisions on the architecture.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Programme views are introduced in the NAF Version 3 to describe the relationships between NATO capability requirements and the various programmes and projects being implemented. They provide programmatic details<br />and highlight the dependencies between capability management and the NATO acquisition process. This information can be further leveraged to show the impact of acquisition decisions on the architecture.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="9ea9c0fe-d4ae-4812-a077-9efc19352422" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990335 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00375: NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint

- page_id: `153990335`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990335/NATO+Service-Oriented+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:43.241+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NSOV is added to the NAF to support building architectures based on the concept of a Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA), which is fundamental to the NNEC paradigm. The NSOV is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the NATO Operational View. A service, within the NSOV, is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer. NSOV focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The view also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behaviour.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NSOV is added to the NAF to support building architectures based on the concept of a Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA), which is fundamental to the NNEC paradigm. The NSOV is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the NATO Operational View. A service, within the NSOV, is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer. NSOV focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The view also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behaviour.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="cae37911-de3e-495e-8809-d1f9df74fe9b" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990347 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00376: NATO Systems Viewpoint

- page_id: `153990347`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990347/NATO+Systems+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:43.416+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NSV is a set of graphical and textual products that describes systems and interconnections providing for or supporting NATO processes. The NSV associates systems resources to the NOV and/or the NSOV. These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the NSOV, and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the NOV.

The views of this view are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NSV is a set of graphical and textual products that describes systems and interconnections providing for or supporting NATO processes. The NSV associates systems resources to the NOV and/or the NSOV. These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the NSOV, and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the NOV.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f2d8bd4-b5bf-4e5b-b114-1604a594cf3c"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qvL33ckmJJY" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>The views of this view are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2c343f17-2404-438d-8e86-392002f17168" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990382 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00377: NATO Technical Viewpoint

- page_id: `153990382`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990382/NATO+Technical+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:44.018+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NTV provides the technical implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based and common building blocks are established. The NTV includes a collection of the technical standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria organized into profiles that govern systems and services, and their elements for a given architecture.

The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The NTV provides the technical implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based and common building blocks are established. The NTV includes a collection of the technical standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria organized into profiles that govern systems and services, and their elements for a given architecture.</p><p>The views of this viewpoint are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d79a3e72-ee4e-4961-b192-01975c9717f7" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990284 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00378: NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information

- page_id: `153990284`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990284/NAV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.634+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO All View Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The overview and summary information contained within the NAV-1 product provides an executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows for quick reference and comparison between architectural descriptions. NAV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture. In an enterprise repository environment, individual architectures are mapped against enterprise phases to provide context between the architectures. NAV-1 is usually a structured text product. Organizations may create a template for the NAV-1 that can then be used to create a consistent set of information across different architecture-based projects.

**Implementation**

NAV-1 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial NAV-1 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.
- Using a NAV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information report

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Architectural Description
- Defines Architecture
- Architecture Metadata

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Generating reports

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
