In an enterprise repository environment, individual architectures are mapped against enterprise phases to provide context between the architectures. NAV-1 is usually a structured text product. Organizations may create a template for the NAV-1 that can then be used to create a consistent set of information across different architecture-based projects.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NAV-1 can be represented:</p><ul><li>Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial NAV-1 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.</li><li>Using a NAV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Nav_1.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Fragment of NAV-1 Overview and Summary Information report</h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="663a06c2-e5d2-4444-93df-5ee25e61bb7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Architectural Description</li><li>Defines Architecture</li><li>Architecture Metadata</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b680ae1c-ec40-4a7d-99fa-26c010ecba2e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Generating reports</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990286 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00379: NAV-2 Integrated Dictionary

- page_id: `153990286`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990286/NAV-2+Integrated+Dictionary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.656+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO All View Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NAV-2 presents all the metadata used in an architecture. An NAV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy and provides a text definition for each one by displaying the following properties:

- The name used for this element in the architecture
- Alternative names for this element, for example, if the element is listed in the NATO ontology it may have multiple names
- A brief description of the element

**Implementation**

NAV-2 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data.
- An AV-2 table. If you have NAV-2 diagrams from previous versions, they will bemigrated into the NAV-1 diagram when loading the project with UPDM 17.0.2.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NAV-2 Integrated Dictionary

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of NAV-2 Integrated Dictionary report

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Definition
- Alias
- Same As

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Generating reports

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990290 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00380: NCV-1 Capability Vision

- page_id: `153990290`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990290/NCV-1+Capability+Vision
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.698+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

NCV-1 addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of Enterprise capabilities. The purpose of an NCV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architecture. It also provides a high-level scope for the Architecture that is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an NOV-1. The Views are high-level and describe capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers (though they may make extensive use of military terminology and acronyms that are clearly defined in the NAV-2 View). It should be noted that visions for capabilities of the enterprise should therefore be placed within entities stereotyped as «EnterpriseVision».

**Implementation**

NCV-1 can be represented using a NCV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### NCV-1 Capability Vision

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Enterprise Goal
- Enterprise Vision
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise
- Structural Part
- Temporal Part
- Enduring Task
- Capability
- Exhibits

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NCV-1 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>NCV-1 addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of Enterprise capabilities. The purpose of an NCV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architecture. It also provides a high-level scope for the Architecture that is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an NOV-1. The Views are high-level and describe capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers (though they may make extensive use of military terminology and acronyms that are clearly defined in the NAV-2 View). It should be noted that visions for capabilities of the enterprise should therefore be placed within entities stereotyped as «EnterpriseVision».</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NCV-1 can be represented using a NCV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ncv_1.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="NCV-1 Capability Vision" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6>NCV-1 Capability Vision</h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d95cbe79-ec41-4f15-a2f4-16d0cd3e81a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Enterprise Goal</li><li>Enterprise Vision</li><li>Enterprise Phase</li><li>Whole-Life Enterprise</li><li>Structural Part</li><li>Temporal Part</li><li>Enduring Task</li><li>Capability</li><li>Exhibits</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6b66700-a15f-4d58-8472-a256f4e8a2b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating NCV-1 diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990292 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00381: NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy

- page_id: `153990292`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990292/NCV-2+Capability+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.719+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-2 Product models capability taxonomies. The view presents a hierarchy of capabilities. These capabilities may be presented in context of an Enterprise Phase, for example, it can show the required capabilities for current and future enterprises.

**Implementation**

NCV-2 can be represented using a NCV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, the oldNCV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Exhibits
- Climate
- Environment
- Environment Property
- Light Condition
- Location

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NCV-2 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The NCV-2 Product models capability taxonomies. The view presents a hierarchy of capabilities. These capabilities may be presented in context of an Enterprise Phase, for example, it can show the required capabilities for current and future enterprises.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NCV-2 can be represented using a NCV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97649483-0ef9-4a78-8af1-23c3eeddb245"><ac:rich-text-body>If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, the oldNCV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ncv_2.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy</h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="212eb01a-0ffd-4337-8280-0cedd6137f98"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Exhibits</li><li>Climate</li><li>Environment</li><li>Environment Property</li><li>Light Condition</li><li>Location</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90bdd683-4b59-443d-96a5-b11a981ccc00"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating NCV-2 diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990294 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00382: NCV-3 Capability Phasing

- page_id: `153990294`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990294/NCV-3+Capability+Phasing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.742+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

NCV-3 addresses the planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time, i.e. capability phasing. NCV-3 Views support the Capability Audit process by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The view indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).

**Implementation**

NCV-3 can be represented using a NCV-3 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### NCV-3 Capability Phasing****

**Related views**

The View is created by analyzing programmatic project data to determine when the projects providing elements of military capability are to be delivered, upgraded and/or withdrawn (this data may be provided in part by a Programme Timelines (NPV-2) View). Then the capability increments identified are structured according to the required capabilities determined in the Capability Taxonomy (NCV-2) View and the Enterprise Phases (from NCV1).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Increment Milestone
- Out Of Service Milestone
- Actual Project

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>NCV-3 addresses the planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time, i.e. capability phasing. NCV-3 Views support the Capability Audit process by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The view indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NCV-3 can be represented using a NCV-3 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ncv_3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="NCV-3 Capability Phasing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6>NCV-3 Capability Phasing<strong><br /></strong></h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The View is created by analyzing programmatic project data to determine when the projects providing elements of military capability are to be delivered, upgraded and/or withdrawn (this data may be provided in part by a Programme Timelines (NPV-2) View). Then the capability increments identified are structured according to the required capabilities determined in the Capability Taxonomy (NCV-2) View and the Enterprise Phases (from NCV1).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9afaa9f-77a4-4c7c-9c8f-acb8c1373694"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Exhibits</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Actual Project</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990296 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00383: NCV-4 Capability Dependencies

- page_id: `153990296`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990296/NCV-4+Capability+Dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.762+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-4 Product describes the dependencies between planned capabilities. It also defines logical groupings of capabilities (capability clusters). The NCV-4 View provides a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management.

**Implementation**

NCV-4 can be represented using a NCV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-4 Capability Dependencies

**Related views**

The Capability Dependencies (NCV-4) View describes the relationships between capabilities (capabilities may be reused from an NCV-2). It also defines logical groupings of capabilities. This contrasts with the NCV-2 which also deals with relationships between Capabilities, but the NCV-2 only addresses the specialization-generalization relationship (for example, capability taxonomy).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Property

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NCV-4 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It also defines logical groupings of capabilities (capability clusters). The NCV-4 View provides a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NCV-4 can be represented using a NCV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ncv_4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="NCV-4 Capability Dependencies" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>NCV-4 Capability Dependencies</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The Capability Dependencies (NCV-4) View describes the relationships between capabilities (capabilities may be reused from an NCV-2). This contrasts with the NCV-2 which also deals with relationships between Capabilities, but the NCV-2 only addresses the specialization-generalization relationship (for example, capability taxonomy).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fb71c80-1d21-4ab4-833f-eb77f1a1827e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Capability Property</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b22b188-6698-45ce-8e03-960d969aad61"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating NCV-4 diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990298 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00384: NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping

- page_id: `153990298`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990298/NCV-5+Capability+to+Organisational+Deployment+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.784+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-5 defines Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping. It addresses the fulfillment of capability requirements, in particular by network enabled capabilities. The NCV-5 View is used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding.

**Implementation**

NCV-5 can be represented using:

- A NCV-5 table. 
 If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, theold NCV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.
- A NCV-5 spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | Capability Phasing is defined in NCV-1. |
|  |  | Capabilities shown in NCV-5 table are usually modeled in NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy. |
|  |  | The timing is based on project milestones from NPV1 which indicate when capability configuration will be delivered to an organization. |
|  |  | Actual Organizational Resources related to deployment of Capability Configurations are taken from NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart. |

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Capability Configuration
- Exhibits
- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Deployed Milestone
- No Longer Used Milestone
- Actual Project
- Enterprise Phase
- Whole-Life Enterprise

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NCV-5 table
- Modifying NCV-5 table

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Deployment Milestones Creation Wizard

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990304 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00385: NCV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping

- page_id: `153990304`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990304/NCV-6+Capability+to+Operational+Activity+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.852+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-6 Product describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the operational activities that those capabilities support.

**Implementation**

NCV-6 can be represented using a NCV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities will be used as row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping

**Related views**

The NCV-6 View provides a bridge between capability analyses using NCVs and operational activities analyzed using NOVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the NSV-5 which maps system functions to operational activities.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Capability
- Standard Operational Activity
- Maps to Capability

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building NCV-6 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The NCV-6 Product describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the operational activities that those capabilities support.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NCV-6 can be represented using a NCV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities will be used as row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as column elements.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Nvc_6.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="NCV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>NCV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The NCV-6 View provides a bridge between capability analyses using NCVs and operational activities analyzed using NOVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the NSV-5 which maps system functions to operational activities.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43c13a43-5d21-45c4-997e-470b6689728a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capability</li><li>Standard Operational Activity</li><li>Maps to Capability</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9a477d4-fd48-4e64-a778-679f9a4d40fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Building NCV-6 matrix</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990306 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00386: NCV-7 Capability to Service Mapping

- page_id: `153990306`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990306/NCV-7+Capability+to+Service+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.873+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NCV-7 Product describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and services as defined for SOA.

**Implementation**

NCV-7 can be represented using a NCV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Service Interfaces will be used as the row elements and the Capabilities will be used as the column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NCV-7 Capability to Service Mapping

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interface
- Expose
- Capability

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Building NCV-7 matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097310434

1097310437

1097310436

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: NA

DoDAF: A needline documents the requirement to exchange information between nodes. The needline does not indicate how the information transfer is implemented.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| exchangedItem : NeedlineExchange[*] | An exchange that occurs on the Needline. |

##### Extensions

UML Association, UML Connector

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990787 space=UPDM2P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00388: New Service Channel wizard

- page_id: `153990787`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990787/New+Service+Channel+wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-18T10:08:31.164+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['new-service-channel-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **New Service Channel** wizard supports the automated Service Oriented modeling in UPDM plugin.

On the basis of input data, the wizard:

- Creates a Service Channel and its required prerequisites.
- Synchronizes Service Parameters with Operational Exchange/ Resource Interaction Items.
- Creates the SV-1 internal diagram to visualize a Service Channel and connected parties.

To open the**New Service Channel** wizard, do one of the following

- Select a Needline and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the New Service Channel button.
- Select a Resource Interface and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the New Service Channel button.
- Select a Service Channel and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the New Service Channel button.
- On the Service Channels Summary table toolbar, click the Add New button.
- On the Service Channels Summary matrix, click the intersection and from the shortcut menu, select New Service Channel .

Dependent on the way the wizard has been opened, it either knows about the Service Provider and the Service Receiver or does not.

The wizard consists of these three steps:

1. Service Channel specification. How to specify a Service Channel?[IMAGE alt='' src='']This step allows you to specify participating parties, service and request ports, and service interfaces.By default Service Provider and Service Requester are required, if you want to finish the wizard in the step #1. You can also choose an existing Service Channel, existing Service and Request points, and Service Interfaces.Service Interfaces can be left undefined. In this case you will not be allowed to proceed further, but you will be able to finish the wizard.Box nameDescriptionService ProviderPerformer/ Resource providing a service for a service requester. If Service Provider is known (the wizard is opened using the smart manipulator on a Needline/ Resource Interface/ System Connector/ Service Channel), it can not be changed. Otherwise, if Service Provider is unknown, it can be selected from the model.Service RequesterPerformer/ Resource requesting a service from a service provider. If Service Requester is known (the wizard is opened using the smart manipulator on Needline/ Resource Interface/ System Connector/ Service Channel), it can not be changed. Otherwise, if Service Requester is unknown, it can be selected from the model.Service ChannelConnector expressing a communication channel between Service Provider and Service Requester. A new Service Channel can be created or the existing one can be selected. Only Service Channels connecting existing and selected Service and Request points can be selected.ServiceAn offer of the Service by Service Provider to Service Receivers. Only Services owned by Service Provider can be selected. If there are none, new Service will be created by default.RequestThe use of a Service by the Service Requester. Only Requests owned by Service Requester can be selected. If there are none, new Request will be created by default.Provider's Service InterfaceThe type of the Service port, which describes interfaces the Service port provides and/or Requires. Any of Service Interfaces can be selected from the model using the drop down list. The field can also be left unspecified. It means you will not be able to specify data/information provided by Service Provider.Requester's Service InterfaceThe type of the Request Point, which describes interfaces the Request Point provides and/or Requires. Any of Service Interfaces can be selected from the model using the drop down list. The field can also be left unspecified. It means you will not be able to specify data/information required by Requester.ButtonDescriptionSwitch Service Provider with Service RequesterThe button is visible only if both Service Provider and Service Requester are known (the wizard is opened using the smart manipulator on a Needline/ Resource Interface/ System Connector/ Service Channel).Click to switch Service Provider with Service Requester.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] (by the**Service Provide** box)The button is visible only if Service Provider is unknown.Click to open the**Select Elements** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] (by the**Service Requester** box)The button is visible only if Service Requester is unknown.Click to open the **Select Elements** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Provider's Service Interface** box)Click to open the element Selection dialog for Provider's Service Interface search/selection/creation.[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Requester's Service Interface** box)Click to open the element Selection dialog for Requester's Service Interface search/selection/creation.
2. Provided Operational Exchange/ Resource Interaction item specification. How to specify a provided exchange Items?[IMAGE alt='' src='']This step allow you to specify provided exchange items that are required by Service Provider and provided by Service Requester in exchange.Check boxDescriptionUse Existing Operational Exchanges/ Resource InteractionsIf selected, application searches for and reuses compatible with provided Data/Information Elements exchanges flowing via Service Provider and Service Requester. Otherwise new exchanges are created instead of reusing existing ones.
3. Required Operational Exchange/ Resource Interaction item specification. How to specify a required exchange Items?[IMAGE alt='' src='']This step allow you to specify required exchange items that are required by Service Provider and provided by Service Requester in exchange.Check boxDescriptionUse Existing Operational Exchanges/ Resource InteractionsIf selected, application searches for and reuses compatible with provided Data/Information Elements exchanges flowing via Service Provider and Service Requester. Otherwise new exchanges are created instead of reusing existing ones. The first step is mandatory and the other steps are optional.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Service Channel Summary Table' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>New Service Channel</strong> wizard supports the automated Service Oriented modeling in UPDM plugin.</p><p>On the basis of input data, the wizard:</p><ul><li>Creates a Service Channel and its required prerequisites.</li><li>Synchronizes Service Parameters with Operational Exchange/ Resource Interaction Items.</li><li>Creates the SV-1 internal diagram to visualize a Service Channel and connected parties.</li></ul><p>To open the<strong> New Service Channel</strong> wizard, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a Needline and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the <strong>New Service Channel</strong> button.</li><li>Select a Resource Interface and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the <strong>New Service Channel</strong> button.</li><li>Select a Service Channel and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the<strong> New Service Channel</strong> button.</li><li>On the Service Channels Summary table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button.</li><li>On the Service Channels Summary matrix, click the intersection and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>New Service Channel</strong>.</li></ul><p>Dependent on the way the wizard has been opened, it either knows about the Service Provider and the Service Receiver or does not.</p><p>The wizard consists of these three steps:</p><ol><li><p>Service Channel specification.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e70466f-6043-40c3-b96c-9ed92abf44da"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a Service Channel?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:title="Specifying Service Channel in New Service Channel wizard" ac:alt="Specifying Service Channel in New Service Channel wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_service_channel_wizard_step1.png" /></ac:image></p><p>This step allows you to specify participating parties, service and request ports, and service interfaces.</p><p>By default Service Provider and Service Requester are required, if you want to finish the wizard in the step #1. You can also choose an existing Service Channel, existing Service and Request points, and Service Interfaces.</p><p>Service Interfaces can be left undefined. In this case you will not be allowed to proceed further, but you will be able to finish the wizard.</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th>Box name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Service Provider</td><td><p>Performer/ Resource providing a service for a service requester. If Service Provider is known (the wizard is opened using the smart manipulator on a Needline/ Resource Interface/ System Connector/ Service Channel), it can not be changed. Otherwise, if Service Provider is unknown, it can be selected from the model.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Service Requester</td><td><p>Performer/ Resource requesting a service from a service provider. If Service Requester is known (the wizard is opened using the smart manipulator on Needline/ Resource Interface/ System Connector/ Service Channel), it can not be changed. Otherwise, if Service Requester is unknown, it can be selected from the model.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Service Channel</td><td><p>Connector expressing a communication channel between Service Provider and Service Requester. A new Service Channel can be created or the existing one can be selected. Only Service Channels connecting existing and selected Service and Request points can be selected.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Service</td><td><p>An offer of the Service by Service Provider to Service Receivers. Only Services owned by Service Provider can be selected. If there are none, new Service will be created by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Request</td><td><p>The use of a Service by the Service Requester. Only Requests owned by Service Requester can be selected. If there are none, new Request will be created by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Provider's Service Interface</td><td><p>The type of the Service port, which describes interfaces the Service port provides and/or Requires. Any of Service Interfaces can be selected from the model using the drop down list. The field can also be left unspecified. It means you will not be able to specify data/information provided by Service Provider.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Requester's Service Interface</p></td><td><p>The type of the Request Point, which describes interfaces the Request Point provides and/or Requires. Any of Service Interfaces can be selected from the model using the drop down list. The field can also be left unspecified. It means you will not be able to specify data/information required by Requester.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p>Switch Service Provider with Service Requester</p></td><td><p>The button is visible only if both Service Provider and Service Requester are known (the wizard is opened using the smart manipulator on a Needline/ Resource Interface/ System Connector/ Service Channel).</p><p>Click to switch Service Provider with Service Requester.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="button_dots.png" /></ac:image> (by the<strong> Service Provide</strong> box)</p></div></td><td><p>The button is visible only if Service Provider is unknown.</p><p>Click to open the<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="button_dots.png" /></ac:image> (by the<strong> Service Requester</strong> box)</p></div></td><td><p>The button is visible only if Service Requester is unknown.</p><p>Click to open the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="button_dots.png" /></ac:image>(by the <strong>Provider's Service Interface</strong> box)</p></div></td><td><p>Click to open the element Selection dialog for Provider's Service Interface search/selection/creation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="button_dots.png" /></ac:image>(by the <strong>Requester's Service Interface</strong> box)</p></div></td><td><p>Click to open the element Selection dialog for Requester's Service Interface search/selection/creation.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Provided Operational Exchange/ Resource Interaction item specification.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3af2d300-fbac-473b-8772-148cf8c04291"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a provided exchange Items?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:title="Specifying provided exchange Items in New Service Channel wizard" ac:alt="Specifying provided exchange Items in New Service Channel wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_service_channel_wizard_step2.png" /></ac:image></p><p>This step allow you to specify provided exchange items that are required by Service Provider and provided by Service Requester in exchange.</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th>Check box</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Use Existing Operational Exchanges/ Resource Interactions</td><td>If selected, application searches for and reuses compatible with provided Data/Information Elements exchanges flowing via Service Provider and Service Requester. Otherwise new exchanges are created instead of reusing existing ones.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Required Operational Exchange/ Resource Interaction item specification.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="055cf5bb-a609-44a6-861a-82b11bfbacbd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify a required exchange Items?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:title="Specifying required exchange Items in New Service Channel wizard" ac:alt="Specifying required exchange Items in New Service Channel wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_service_channel_wizard_step3.png" /></ac:image></p><p>This step allow you to specify required exchange items that are required by Service Provider and provided by Service Requester in exchange.</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th>Check box</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Use Existing Operational Exchanges/ Resource Interactions</td><td>If selected, application searches for and reuses compatible with provided Data/Information Elements exchanges flowing via Service Provider and Service Requester. Otherwise new exchanges are created instead of reusing existing ones.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1cb7191e-e182-4554-b2ab-bda7f057ff4d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The first step is mandatory and the other steps are optional.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aef28ee6-3299-48a5-b9fa-a4020ebcd43d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Service Channel Summary Table" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990612 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00389: No Longer Used Milestone

- page_id: `153990612`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990612/No+Longer+Used+Milestone
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:40:52.496+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Capability Viewpoint and Strategic Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915485182

915485185

915485184

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that an ActualOrganisationResource ceased to use or is slated to cease using a CapabilityConfiguration from a specific point in time. This is used to describe capabilities going out of service with specific organizations or posts.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| noLongerUsedBy : ActualOrganizationalResource[1..*] | ActualOrganizationalResources that are no longer using CapabilityConfiguration that went out of service at this Milestone. |
| configuration : CapabilityConfiguration[1..*] | Affected CapabilityConfigurations. |

##### Extensions

UML Instance Specification

##### Related DoDAF views

[CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/CV-5+Capability+to+Organizational+Development+Mapping)

[SV-8 Systems Evolution Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-8+Systems+Evolution+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+StV-5+Capability+to+Organization+Deployment+Mapping)

[SV-8 Systems Evolution Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-8+Systems+Evolution+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NCV-5+Capability+to+Organisational+Deployment+Mapping)

[NSV-8 System Evolution Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NSV-8+System+Evolution+Description)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6919b1c2-1372-4cde-9e1c-2bdd5ab89072"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">915485182</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1a75982-70e2-4190-9528-a2bfd966c024"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">915485185</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5abee95-42ea-4612-8a94-5de8dba68da1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">915485184</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span>Description</span></h3><p><span>MODAF/NAF: Asserts that an ActualOrganisationResource ceased to use or is slated to cease using a CapabilityConfiguration from a specific point in time. This is used to describe capabilities going out of service with specific organizations or posts.</span></p><h3><span>Architecture Framework</span></h3><p><span>MODAF, DoDAF, NAF</span></p><h3><span>Properties</span></h3><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><span>noLongerUsedBy : ActualOrganizationalResource[1..*]</span></p></td><td><p><span>ActualOrganizationalResources that are no longer using CapabilityConfiguration that went out of service at this Milestone.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span>configuration : CapabilityConfiguration[1..*]</span></p></td><td><p><span>Affected CapabilityConfigurations.</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><span>Extensions</span></h3><p><span>UML Instance Specification</span></p><h3>Related DoDAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/CV-5+Capability+to+Organizational+Development+Mapping">CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping</a></p><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-8+Systems+Evolution+Description">SV-8 Systems Evolution Description</a></p><h3>Related MODAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+StV-5+Capability+to+Organization+Deployment+Mapping">MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping</a></p><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-8+Systems+Evolution+Description">SV-8 Systems Evolution Description</a></p><h3>Related NAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NCV-5+Capability+to+Organisational+Deployment+Mapping">NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping</a></p><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NSV-8+System+Evolution+Description">NSV-8 System Evolution Description</a></p><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00390: Node

- page_id: `153990667`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990667/Node
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:26:26.121+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097338417

1097338420

1097338418

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A Node (MODAF::NodeType, NAF::NodeType) is a logical entity that performs operational activities.

Nodes are specified independently of any physical realization.

DoDAF: A Node (DoDAF::OperationalNode) is an element of the operational architecture that produces, consumes, or processes information.

This is also a specialization of Performer.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related MODAF views

## PAGE 00391: Node Port

- page_id: `153990668`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990668/Node+Port
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:42:05.983+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1097398545

1097398548

1097398547

##### Description

UPDM: A port is a property of a Node that specifies a distinct interaction point between the node and its environment or between the (behavior of the) node and its internal parts. It is the “entry/exit” point where resources (e.g., energy, information/data and people, etc.) flow in and out of a node.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Port

##### Related DoDAF views

[OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/OV-2+Operational+Resource+Flow+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+OV-2+Operational+Node+Relationship+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-2+Operational+Node+Connectivity+Description)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990315 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00393: NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description

- page_id: `153990315`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990315/NOV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.970+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of High-level Operational Concept Description is to provide a high-level graphical and textual description of operational concept (high level organizations, missions, geographic configuration, connectivity, etc) of what the architecture is supposed to do, and how it is supposed to do it. The NOV-1, along with the corresponding NAV-1 product is intended to serve as an executive summary of the architecture.

**Implementation**

NOV-1 can be represented using:

- A link to an external document.
- An NOV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- An NOV-1 Free Form diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure Diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- High-Level Operational Concept
- Concept Role
- Arbitrary Relationship

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NOV-1 diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The purpose of High-level Operational Concept Description is to provide a high-level graphical and textual description of operational concept (high level organizations, missions, geographic configuration, connectivity, etc) of what the architecture is supposed to do, and how it is supposed to do it. The NOV-1, along with the corresponding NAV-1 product is intended to serve as an executive summary of the architecture.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NOV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A link to an external document.</li><li>An NOV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>An NOV-1 Free Form diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>A UML Composite Structure Diagram.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Nov_1_high_level.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic</h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65ac8bbb-81d4-4b03-945b-3dc1754f0e0d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>High-Level Operational Concept</li><li>Concept Role</li><li>Arbitrary Relationship</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6ad6e1f-0cd1-4f55-895d-a78e700a61a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating NOV-1 diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990317 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00394: NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description

- page_id: `153990317`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990317/NOV-2+Operational+Node+Connectivity+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:42.994+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Operational Node Connectivity Description is intended to track the need to exchange information from specific operational nodes (that play a key role in the architecture) to others. An NOV-2 does not depict the connectivity between the nodes. NAF modifies the NOV-2 in two ways. First it recommends that an NOV-2 diagram shows the platforms or geographic locations at which operational nodes are deployed. Secondly it provides additional information about each needline in the form of a requirements specification. There are now four types of needlines identified as follows:

1. InformationExchange.
2. EnergyFlow.
3. MaterielFlow.
4. MovementOfPeople.

In addition, NAF permits service-oriented architectures. Instead of needlines between nodes, it is possible simply to show which services the nodes provide and consume. Finally, NAF again permits known resources to be shown in a NOV-2. However, this must be clearly shown as a KnownResource in a NOV-2 model. LogicalArchitecture, which is the container class for all the nodes and KnownResources, is introduced.

**Implementation**

NOV-2 can be represented using:

- An NOV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- An NOV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NOV-2 Operational Node Internal Connectivity Description

**Related views**

An NOV-2 is highly related with an NOV-5. Operational Nodes shown in the NOV-2 are the performers of the Operational Activities modeled in the NOV-5. NOV-2 focuses on the Operational Nodes, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The NOV-5, on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Nodes, which can be shown as annotations or swim-lanes on the activities.

Information flows can be modeled either in the NOV-2 or NOV-5. In both cases they are highly associated and in general should be reused between these views.

The NOV-2 displays the Capabilities required by Nodes from NCV-2. That is an association between two abstraction levels of user requirements where the NOV is more specific than the StV and a Node is more specific concept than a Capability.

The other important mapping is between NOV-2 and NSV-1. The specification Node and implementation Resource are subjects to map here. One NOV-2 product can have several implementations in the NSV-1.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Node
- Logical Architecture
- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element
- Node Port
- Node Role
- Needline
- Capability
- Exhibits
- Actual Location
- Location Type
- Energy
- Materiel
- Is Capable Of Performing
- Operational Activity
- Known Resource
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Problem Domain
- Security Domain
- Service Channel
- Service
- Property
- Request

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NOV-2 diagram
- Creating Operational Exchanges in NOV-2 diagram

#### PANEL: Related references

Related references

- Operational Exchange Creation Wizard
- Operational Exchange Manager Dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Operational Node Connectivity Description is intended to track the need to exchange information from specific operational nodes (that play a key role in the architecture) to others. An NOV-2 does not depict the connectivity between the nodes. NAF modifies the NOV-2 in two ways. First it recommends that an NOV-2 diagram shows the platforms or geographic locations at which operational nodes are deployed. Secondly it provides additional information about each needline in the form of a requirements specification. There are now four types of needlines identified as follows:</p><ol><li>InformationExchange.</li><li>EnergyFlow.</li><li>MaterielFlow.</li><li><p>MovementOfPeople.</p></li></ol><p>In addition, NAF permits service-oriented architectures. Instead of needlines between nodes, it is possible simply to show which services the nodes provide and consume. Finally, NAF again permits known resources to be shown in a NOV-2. However, this must be clearly shown as a KnownResource in a NOV-2 model. LogicalArchitecture, which is the container class for all the nodes and KnownResources, is introduced.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NOV-2 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>An NOV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>An NOV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>A UML Class diagram.</li><li>A UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>A SysML Block Definition diagram.</li><li>A SysML Internal Block diagram.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Nov_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description</h6><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Nov_2_example.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>NOV-2 Operational Node Internal Connectivity Description</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>An NOV-2 is highly related with an NOV-5. Operational Nodes shown in the NOV-2 are the performers of the Operational Activities modeled in the NOV-5. NOV-2 focuses on the Operational Nodes, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The NOV-5, on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Nodes, which can be shown as annotations or swim-lanes on the activities.</p><p>Information flows can be modeled either in the NOV-2 or NOV-5. In both cases they are highly associated and in general should be reused between these views. </p><p>The NOV-2 displays the Capabilities required by Nodes from NCV-2. That is an association between two abstraction levels of user requirements where the NOV is more specific than the StV and a Node is more specific concept than a Capability.</p><p>The other important mapping is between NOV-2 and NSV-1. The specification Node and implementation Resource are subjects to map here. One NOV-2 product can have several implementations in the NSV-1.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a761aa7-58e0-4906-a930-2ba255e55eee"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Node</li><li>Logical Architecture</li><li>Operational Exchange</li><li>Exchange Element</li><li>Node Port</li><li>Node Role</li><li>Needline</li><li>Capability</li><li>Exhibits</li><li>Actual Location</li><li>Location Type</li><li>Energy</li><li>Materiel</li><li>Is Capable Of Performing</li><li>Operational Activity</li><li>Known Resource</li><li>Resource Artifact</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization</li><li>Post</li><li>Problem Domain</li><li>Security Domain</li><li>Service Channel</li><li>Service</li><li>Property</li><li>Request</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d6beabc-739a-4cb9-ae5d-04115f782c1b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating NOV-2 diagram</li><li>Creating Operational Exchanges in NOV-2 diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75b03eac-9b7b-4cb5-9fe6-7d15768d6e9d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related references</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Creation Wizard</li><li>Operational Exchange Manager Dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990320 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00395: NOV-3 Operational Information Requirements

- page_id: `153990320`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990320/NOV-3+Operational+Information+Requirements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:43.044+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Information exchanges express the relationship across the three basic architecture data elements of an NOV (operational activities, operational nodes, and information flow) with a focus on the specific aspects of the information flow and the information content.

The Information Exchanges of the NOV-3 should remain at a high level of aggregation to represent actual information workflow products that are used at the operational nodes shown in the NOV-2 (and not their subordinate operational nodes).

**Implementation**

NOV-3 can be represented using:

- An NOV-3 table.
- An NOV-3 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Node Roles(node usages).
- An NOV-3 spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NOV-3 Operational Information Requirements

**Related views**

An NOV-3 is initially constructed from the information contained in the NATO Operational Node Connectivity Description (NOV-2).

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Node
- Operational Exchange
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Materiel
- Resource Artifact
- Post
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Operational Activity

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NOV-3 table
- Displaying Custom Measurements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990322 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00396: NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart

- page_id: `153990322`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990322/NOV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-04T11:08:43.068+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The NATO Organizational Relationships Chart illustrates the command structure or relationships (as opposed to relationships with respect to a business process flow) among human roles, organizations, or organization types that are the key players in architecture. NAF divides The NOV-4 in two views: an NOV-4 Typical and an NOV-4 Actual.

**Implementation**

NOV-4 can be represented using:

- An NOV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Organization
- Post
- Person
- Command
- Competence
- Provides Competence
- Requires Competence
- Actual Organization
- Actual Post
- Actual Person
- Fills Post
- Actual Organization Relationship
- Actual Organization Role
- Operational Activity
- Owns Process

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- Creating NOV-4 diagram
- Instantiating Structures

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The NATO Organizational Relationships Chart illustrates the command structure or relationships (as opposed to relationships with respect to a business process flow) among human roles, organizations, or organization types that are the key players in architecture. NAF divides The NOV-4 in two views: an NOV-4 Typical and an NOV-4 Actual.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>NOV-4 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>An NOV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>A UML Class diagram.</li><li>A SysML Block Definition diagram.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Nov_4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart</h6></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89e32fad-dceb-4269-a9aa-910a0ced46d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Organization</li><li>Post</li><li>Person</li><li>Command</li><li>Competence</li><li>Provides Competence</li><li>Requires Competence</li><li>Actual Organization</li><li>Actual Post</li><li>Actual Person</li><li>Fills Post</li><li>Actual Organization Relationship</li><li>Actual Organization Role</li><li>Operational Activity</li><li>Owns Process</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0758c371-2452-439f-9284-484488fb181a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Creating NOV-4 diagram</li><li>Instantiating Structures</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
