<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990534 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00461: P8 Resource Constraints

- page_id: `153990534`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990534/P8+Resource+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T08:43:27.658+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['p8-resource-constraints', 'p8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The P8 Resource Constraints view specifies functional and non-functional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (i.e. the structural and behavioral elements of the Resource viewpoint). It constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the Resource architecture. The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e. non-functional).

The P8 view may be used to:

- Define implementation logic.
- Identify resource constraints.

**Implementation**

The P8 view can be represented using:

- A P8 Resource Constraints table. [ATTACHMENT filename='P8.PNG']

- A P8 spreadsheet report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P8 table' space='UPDM2P2024x']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Constraint
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Function
- Entity Item
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

##### Description

UPDM: A type of a human being that is recognized by law as the subject of rights and duties. This is used to define the characteristics that require capturing for ActualPersons (e.g., properties such as address, rank, telephone number, etc.).

MODAF/NAF: NA

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

## PAGE 00466: Physical Data Model

- page_id: `153990726`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

476178045

476178048

476178046

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A PhysicalDataModel is an implementable specification of a data structure. A PhysicalDataModel realizes a LogicalDataModel, taking into account implementation restrictions and performance issues whilst still enforcing the constraints, relationships and typing of the logical model.

DoDAF: A Physical Data Model defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architecture.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Package

##### Related DoDAF views

[DIV-3 Physical Data Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/DIV-3+Physical+Data+Model)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-11 Physical Schema](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+SV-11+Physical+Schema)

##### Related NAF views

[NSV-11 Systems Data Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/NSV-11+Systems+Data+Model)

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2778058-72d8-48df-88f1-8498baec2fe0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">476178045</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ccb6699-d37f-4fd0-a834-d99323a562ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">476178048</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="72a1a173-71b7-4bef-9bc3-fd6b0113a554"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">476178046</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span>Description</span></h3><p><span>MODAF/NAF: A PhysicalDataModel is an implementable specification of a data structure. A PhysicalDataModel realizes a LogicalDataModel, taking into account implementation restrictions and performance issues whilst still enforcing the constraints, relationships and typing of the logical model.</span></p><p><span>DoDAF: A Physical Data Model defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architecture.</span></p><h3><span>Architecture Framework</span></h3><p><span>MODAF, DoDAF, NAF</span></p><h3><span>Extensions</span></h3><p><span>UML Package</span></p><h3>Related DoDAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/DIV-3+Physical+Data+Model">DIV-3 Physical Data Model</a></p><h3>Related MODAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+SV-11+Physical+Schema">MODAF. SV-11 Physical Schema</a></p><h3>Related NAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/NSV-11+Systems+Data+Model">NSV-11 Systems Data Model</a></p><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990492 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00467: Physical Resource viewpoint

- page_id: `153990492`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990492/Physical+Resource+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T10:12:32.232+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['physical-resource-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Physical Resource viewpoint is a set of graphical and textual products that describes systems and interconnections providing for or supporting NATO processes. This viewpoint associates systems resources to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logical viewpoint' space='UPDM2P2024x'] and/or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service viewpoint' space='UPDM2P2024x']. These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service viewpoint' space='UPDM2P2024x'], and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logical viewpoint' space='UPDM2P2024x'].

#### PANEL: Views

Views

1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

These systems are the resources that are used to construct the services of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Service viewpoint" /></ac:link>, and support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information among operational nodes as defined in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Logical viewpoint" /></ac:link>.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00468: Post

- page_id: `153990686`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990686/Post
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:12:56.568+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1100808206

1100808208

1100808207

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A Post (MODAF::PostType, NAF::PostType) is a type of point of contact or responsible person. Note that this is the type of post, e.g., Desk Officer, Commander Land Component, etc.

DoDAF: A Post (DoDAF::PersonType) is a category of persons defined by the role or roles they share that are relevant to an architecture.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990537 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00469: Pr Configuration Management

- page_id: `153990537`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990537/Pr+Configuration+Management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-17T08:38:20.362+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint
- labels: ['pr-configuration-management', 'pr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Pr Configuration Management view depicts the whole lifecycle view of a Resource, describing how its configuration changes over time. The Pr view provides an overview of how a capability configuration structure changes over time. It shows the structure of different versions of Resources (usually Capability Configurations or Service Implementations) mapped against a timeline.

The Pr view may be used for:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The Pr view can be represented using:

- A Pr Configuration Management diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- A Pr MS Word document report.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Pr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Version of Configurations in Pr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Resources in Pr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing Resources from Pr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding Version of Configurations in Pr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Whole Life Configuration
- Version Of Configuration
- Resource Artifact
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization
- Post
- Actual Project Milestone
- Increment Milestone
- Out Of Service Milestone

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Milestone</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990945 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00470: Predefined Metric Suites

- page_id: `153990945`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990945/Predefined+Metric+Suites
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-27T14:22:36.390+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Coverage Analysis
- labels: ['metric-suites', 'capabilities-covered-by-nodes', 'capabilities-covered-by-opactivities', 'nodes-covered-by-sysresources', 'opactivitie-covered-by-functions', 'opactivities-covered-by-nodes', 'capabilities-covered-by-sysinterfaces', 'capabilities-covered-by-capconfiguration', 'capabilities-covered-by-projects', 'orgresources-covered-by-projects', 'projects-covered-by-milestones', 'functions-covered-by-sysresources']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Metric suite is a collection of parameters and metric definitions used to calculate metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model.

For your convenience, there are eleven predefined metric suites. See the following figure for better understanding of the predefined metric suites and their purposes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Capabilities Covered by Nodes**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Nodes" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Nodes in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Nodes, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Capabilities Covered by OpActivities**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by OpActivities" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Activities in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Activities, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Nodes Covered by SysResources**

The Metric Suite "Nodes Covered by SysResources" counts the percentage of the Nodes covered by System Resources in the particular package. The number of Nodes, System Resources, and the coverage average are also counted.

**OpActivities Covered by Functions**

The Metric Suite "OpActivities Covered by Functions" counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Functions in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Functions, and the coverage average are also counted.

**OpActivities Covered by Nodes**

The Metric Suite "OpActivities Covered by Nodes" counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Nodes in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Nodes, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Capabilities Covered by SysInterfaces**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by SysInterfaces" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by System Interfaces in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, System Interfaces, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Capabilities Covered by CapConfiguration**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by CapConfiguration" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures, and the coverage average are also counted.

**Capabilities Covered by Projects**

The Metric Suite "Capabilities Covered by Projects" counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.

**OrgResources Covered by Projects**

The Metric Suite "OrgResources Covered by Projects" counts the percentage of the Organization Resources covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Organization Resources, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.

****

**Projects Covered by Milestones**

The Metric Suite "Projects Covered by Milestones" counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Milestones in the particular package. The number of Actual Projects, Actual Milestones, and the coverage average are also counted.

****

**Functions Covered by SysResources**

The Metric Suite "Functions Covered by SysResources" counts the percentage of the Functions covered by the System Resources in the particular package. The number of Functions, System Resources, and the coverage average are also counted

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Metric suite is a collection of parameters and metric definitions used to calculate metrics that evaluate specific aspects of your model.</p><p>For your convenience, there are eleven predefined metric suites. See the following figure for better understanding of the predefined metric suites and their purposes.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Metric Suite hierarchy" ac:title="Metric Suite hierarchy"><ri:attachment ri:filename="metric_suite_hierarchy.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Predefined Metric Suites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by Nodes</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Nodes&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Nodes in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Nodes, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by OpActivities</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by OpActivities&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Operational Activities in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Operational Activities, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Nodes Covered by SysResources</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Nodes Covered by SysResources&quot; counts the percentage of the Nodes covered by System Resources in the particular package. The number of Nodes, System Resources, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>OpActivities Covered by Functions</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;OpActivities Covered by Functions&quot; counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Functions in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Functions, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>OpActivities Covered by Nodes</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;OpActivities Covered by Nodes&quot; counts the percentage of the Operational Activities covered by Nodes in the particular package. The number of Operational Activities, Nodes, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by SysInterfaces</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by SysInterfaces&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by System Interfaces in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, System Interfaces, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by CapConfiguration</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by CapConfiguration&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Capability Configurations or Physical Architectures, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>Capabilities Covered by Projects</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Capabilities Covered by Projects&quot; counts the percentage of the Capabilities covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Capabilities, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p> </p><p><strong>OrgResources Covered by Projects</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;OrgResources Covered by Projects&quot; counts the percentage of the Organization Resources covered by the Actual Projects in the particular package. The number of Organization Resources, Actual Projects, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Projects Covered by Milestones</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Projects Covered by Milestones&quot; counts the percentage of the Actual Projects covered by the Actual Milestones in the particular package. The number of Actual Projects, Actual Milestones, and the coverage average are also counted.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Functions Covered by SysResources</strong></p><p>The Metric Suite &quot;Functions Covered by SysResources&quot; counts the percentage of the Functions covered by the System Resources in the particular package. The number of Functions, System Resources, and the coverage average are also counted</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990572 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00471: Predefined Relation Maps

- page_id: `153990572`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990572/Predefined+Relation+Maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-05-15T22:02:19.290+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Supportive Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Relation Map diagram enables you to review and analyze relations rapidly between the elements of the whole model. Our modeling tool presents you a number of predefined relation maps. You will be able to change the context and scope only. You can change the context by dragging the selected element onto the map.

The predefined relation maps are as follows:

##### Capability Structural Map

A Capability Structural Map depicts the structural relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:

- the Capability itself
- related DoDAF Performers (or MODAF Nodes)
- related concrete System Resources including Capability Configurations, Organization Types, Software System Resources

A Capability Structural Map also presents the internal compositions of each mapped element as well as the relationships among these elements.

##### Capability Behavioral Map

A Capability Behavioral Map depicts the behavioral relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:

- the Capability itself
- related Operational Activities
- related System Functions

A Capability Behavioral Map also presents the internal decomposition of each mapped behavioral element as well as the relationships among these elements.

##### Implementation Map

The Implementation Map depicts the implementation relationships of a DoDAF Performer (or of a MODAF Node). The predefined map includes either the DoDAF Performer (or the MODAF Node) and the concrete System Resource(s) that has (have) been indicated as implementing that operational element. For example, the Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software element that implements the Performer or Node. When the Performer or Node has composite structure and when the System Resources have composite structure, the Implementation Map also includes the implementation elements for the composed elements.

##### Operational Activity Implementation Map

The Operational Activity Implementation Map depicts the behavioral implementation of an Operational Activity, relating the Operational Activity and the Systems Functions that implement the operational behavior. When the Operational Activity has composite behaviors and when the System Functions have composite functionality, the Operational Activity Implementation Map also includes the behavioral elements for the composed elements.

##### Operational Activity Map

The Operational Activity Map depicts the behavioral decomposition of an Operational Activity.

##### Organization Structure Map

The Organization Structure Map depicts the structural composition of an Actual Organization. The predefined map includes: the Actual Organization itself, DoDAF Individual Person Roles (or MODAF Actual Posts), and Actual Person elements. The Organization Structure Map illustrates the relationships between these organizational elements.

##### System Resource Map

The System Resource Map depicts the structural decomposition of a concrete System Resource-such as a Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software-element.

##### Variant Map

The Variant Map depicts concrete specializations of an abstract System Resource such as the direct specializations Service Access, Capability Configuration, and Software and any other specializations of these that appear in the modeled generalization hierarchy.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Relation Map diagram enables you to review and analyze relations rapidly between the elements of the whole model. Our modeling tool presents you a number of predefined relation maps. You will be able to change the context and scope only. You can change the context by dragging the selected element onto the map.</p><p>The predefined relation maps are as follows:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2c7cce1-4f9b-4604-9e6e-3ac3b551926b" /></p><h3>Capability Structural Map</h3><p><span>A Capability Structural Map depicts the structural relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:</span></p><ul><li>the Capability itself</li><li>related DoDAF Performers (or MODAF Nodes)</li><li><span>related concrete System Resources including Capability Configurations, Organization Types, </span>Software System Resources</li></ul><p><span>A Capability Structural Map also presents the internal compositions of each mapped element as well as the relationships among these elements.</span></p><h3>Capability Behavioral Map</h3><p><span>A Capability Behavioral Map depicts the behavioral relationships of a Capability. The predefined map includes:</span></p><ul><li><span>the Capability itself</span></li><li>related Operational Activities</li><li>related System Functions</li></ul><p><span>A Capability Behavioral Map also presents the internal decomposition of each mapped behavioral element as well as the relationships among these elements.</span></p><h3>Implementation Map</h3><p><span>The Implementation Map depicts the implementation relationships of a DoDAF Performer (or of a MODAF Node). The predefined map includes either the DoDAF Performer (or the MODAF Node) and the concrete System Resource(s) that has (have) been indicated as implementing that operational element. For example, the Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software element that implements the Performer or Node. When the Performer or Node has composite structure and when the System Resources have composite structure, the Implementation Map also includes the implementation elements for the composed elements.</span></p><h3>Operational Activity Implementation Map</h3><p><span>The Operational Activity Implementation Map depicts the behavioral implementation of an Operational Activity, relating the Operational Activity and the Systems Functions that implement the operational behavior. When the Operational Activity has composite behaviors and when the System Functions have composite functionality, the Operational Activity Implementation Map also includes the behavioral elements for the composed elements.</span></p><h3>Operational Activity Map</h3><p><span>The Operational Activity Map depicts the behavioral decomposition of an Operational Activity.</span></p><h3>Organization Structure Map</h3><p><span>The Organization Structure Map depicts the structural composition of an Actual Organization. The predefined map includes: the Actual Organization itself, DoDAF Individual Person Roles (or MODAF Actual Posts), and Actual Person elements. The Organization Structure Map illustrates the relationships between these organizational elements.</span></p><h3>System Resource Map</h3><p><span>The System Resource Map depicts the structural decomposition of a concrete System Resource-such as a Capability Configuration, Organization Type, or Software-element.</span></p><h3>Variant Map</h3><p><span>The Variant Map depicts concrete specializations of an abstract System Resource such as the direct specializations Service Access, Capability Configuration, and Software and any other specializations of these that appear in the modeled generalization hierarchy.</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00472: Problem Domain

- page_id: `153990687`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990687/Problem+Domain
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:26:06.845+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1100860662

1100860664

1100860663

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: The boundary containing those Nodes which may be realized by functional resources specified in SV-1. There may be more than one alternative solution for a given ProblemDomain specified as a set of SV suites. There may be only one ProblemDomain in a LogicalArchitecture.

DoDAF: NA – covered by the more general temporalWholePart element.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Property

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+OV-2+Operational+Node+Relationship+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-2+Operational+Node+Connectivity+Description)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990793 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00474: Producing and Consuming Activities dialog

- page_id: `153990793`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990793/Producing+and+Consuming+Activities+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-26T15:29:01.714+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['producing-and-consuming-activities-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Producing and Consuming Activities** dialog allows you to specify or modify producing and consuming Activities for a selected Operational Exchange.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Producing and Consuming Activities** dialog

- In the Specification window of the Operational Exchange, click the Edit (...) button in the selected Producing Activity or Consuming Activity property value cell.
- In the OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix, click the Edit (...) button in the selected Producing Operational Activity cell.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024x']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog : - Click the **Edit** (...) button in the selected **Producing Operational Activity** or **Consuming Operational Activity** cell. - Click the **Activities** button.

Elements of the dialog are described in the following tables.

| Box | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Update Diagrams | Select to update all related diagrams. Existing diagrams will be updated by displaying a particular operational exchange as a triangle on every Operational Activity Edge connecting producing and consuming activity actions. If the specified Producing and Consuming Activities does not exist in any diagram, you will be suggested to create a new OV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model (DoDAF), OV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model (MODAF), or NOV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model (NAF) diagram. Producing and Consuming Activity actions and the Operational Exchange will be represented in this new diagram. |

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add | Click to add a new row for assigning a pair of Producing and Consuming Activities. |
| Remove From Table | Click to delete a selected Producing and Consuming Activities pair. |
| Show Full Paths | Click to show views containing specified activities (see the following figure).Click once again to hide this information. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To specify a pair of producing and consuming activities

1. In the Producing Operational Activity column of a selected row, create a new value:
  - Click the drop-down arrow to open a list of available activities and select one. If you do not see the list of available functions, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the**Performed Activities** button.
  - Click the “ ... ” button to create a new or select an existing activity from the model. The Select Operational Activity dialog opens, wherein you are able to select or create activities.
2. Perform the same action described above for the **Consuming Operational Activity** column of the same row.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering Operational Activities and Functions' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Producing and Consuming Activities</strong> dialog allows you to specify or modify producing and consuming Activities for a selected Operational Exchange.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Producing and Consuming Activities dialog" ac:title="Producing and Consuming Activities dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="producing_consuming_activities_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Producing and Consuming Activities dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To open the <strong>Producing and Consuming Activities</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Specification window of the Operational Exchange, click the <strong>Edit</strong> (...) button in the selected <strong>Producing Activity or Consuming Activity</strong> property value cell.</li><li>In the OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix, click the <strong>Edit</strong> (...) button in the selected <strong>Producing Operational Activity</strong> cell.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>:<br /><p>- Click the <strong>Edit</strong> (...) button in the selected <strong>Producing Operational Activity</strong> or <strong>Consuming Operational Activity</strong> cell.</p><p>- Click the <strong>Activities</strong> button.</p></li></ul><p>Elements of the dialog are described in the following tables.</p><table><tbody><tr><th>Box</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Update Diagrams</td><td><p>Select to update all related diagrams. Existing diagrams will be updated by displaying a particular operational exchange as a triangle on every Operational Activity Edge connecting producing and consuming activity actions. If the specified Producing and Consuming Activities does not exist in any diagram, you will be suggested to create a new OV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model (DoDAF), OV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model (MODAF), or NOV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model (NAF) diagram. Producing and Consuming Activity actions and the Operational Exchange will be represented in this new diagram.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><table><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Add</td><td><p>Click to add a new row for assigning a pair of Producing and Consuming Activities.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Remove From Table</td><td>Click to delete a selected Producing and Consuming Activities pair.</td></tr><tr><td>Show Full Paths</td><td><p>Click to show views containing specified activities (see the following figure).</p><p>Click once again to hide this information.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><ac:image ac:alt="Show Full Paths button in Producing and Consuming Activities dialog" ac:title="Show Full Paths button in Producing and Consuming Activities dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_full_types_button_in_producing_consuming_activities_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Producing and Consuming Activities dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To specify a pair of producing and consuming activities</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the Producing Operational Activity column of a selected row, create a new value:</p><ul><li><p>Click the drop-down arrow to open a list of available activities and select one.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="017e31a3-8710-4745-b34e-af7b16e45360" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not see the list of available functions, click <ac:image ac:alt="Filter Options" ac:title="Filter Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select  the<strong> Performed Activities</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the “<strong>...</strong>” button to create a new or select an existing activity from the model. The <strong>Select Operational Activity</strong> dialog opens, wherein you are able to select or create activities.</li></ul></li><li><p> Perform the same action described above for the <strong>Consuming Operational Activity</strong> column of the same row.</p></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="6155de8f-0747-45ec-a574-288c84d1be43" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Filtering Operational Activities and Functions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990796 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00475: Producing and Consuming Functions dialog

- page_id: `153990796`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990796/Producing+and+Consuming+Functions+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-26T15:17:06.388+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['producing-and-consuming-functions-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog allows you to specify or modify producing and consuming Functions for a selected Resource Interaction.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog

- In the Specification window of the Resource Interaction, click the Edit (...) button in the selected Producing Function or Consuming Function cell.
- In the SV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix, click the Edit (...) button in the selected Producing Function or Consuming Function cell.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024x']**Resource Interaction Manager dialog** : - Click the **Edit** (...) button in the selected **Producing Function or Consuming Function** cell. - Click the **Functions** button.

The **Producing and Consuming Functions** dialog is embedded in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024x']**Realized Resource Interaction** wizardas a step #2.

Elements of the dialog are described in the following tables.

| Box | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Update Diagrams | Select to update all related diagrams. Existing diagrams will be updated by displaying a particular resource interaction as a triangle on every Function Edge connecting producing and consuming function actions. If the specified Producing and Consuming Functions does not exist in any diagram, you will be suggested to create a new SV-4 System Functionality Description Flow (DoDAF), SV-4 Functionality Description Flow (MODAF), or NSV-4 System Functionality Description Flow (NAF) diagram. Producing and Consuming Function actions and the Resource Interaction will be represented in this new diagram. |

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add | Click to add a new row for assigning a pair of Producing and Consuming Functions. |
| Remove From Table | Click to delete a selected Producing and Consuming Functions pair. |
| Show Full Paths | Click to show views containing specified functions (see the following figure).Click once again to hide this information. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To specify a pair of producing and consuming functions

1. In the**Producing Function** column of a selected row, create a new value:
  - Click the drop-down arrow to open a list of available functions and select one. If you do not see the list of available functions, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the **Performed Functions** button.
  - Click the “ ... ” button to create a new or select a function existing in the model. The Select Function dialog opens, wherein you are able to select or create functions.
2. Perform the same action described above for the **Consuming Function** column of the same row.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering Operational Activities and Functions' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interaction Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A Project (MODAF::ProjectType, NAF::ProjectType) is used to define a category of project: For example, “Program”, “Acquisition Project” or “Training Program”.

DoDAF: NA (only Individual Project in DoDAF).

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

1339329081

1339329084

1339329083

##### Description

UPDM: An element representing a collection of themes (e.g., DLOD or DOTMLPF) which is connected to a Project as part of a Project’s definition. This is used as a template for ActualProjectMilestones.

MODAF/NAF: An event in a Project by which progress is measured.

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

DoDAF: NA

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A ProjectStatus (MODAF::StatusAtMilestone, NAF::StatusAtMilestone) is a relationship between a Status and a milestone that asserts the status (i.e., level of progress) of a ProjectTheme for the project at the time of the ActualProjectMilestone (MODAF::Milestone, NAF::Milestone).

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Slot

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990799 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00483: Project Status Application Wizard

- page_id: `153990799`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990799/Project+Status+Application+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T10:55:34.121+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['project-status-application-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Project Status Application Wizard** is used to apply the DLOD Milestone, DOTMLPF Milestone, or custom Milestone type to the selected element, which can be one of the following:

- Out Of Service Milestone
- Increment Milestone
- Deployed Milestone
- No Longer Used Milestone
- Actual Project Milestone

To open the **Project Status Application Wizard**

1. Right-click the Milestone and select Edit Status . The Project Status Application Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of two steps:

1. Select an Actual Project Milestone Type. How to select an Actual Project Milestone Type?In this step, select DLOD Milestone, DOTMLPF Milestone, or create your own Milestone type.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Specify Project Status at a particular Milestone. How to specify Project Status at a particular Milestone?In this step, specify project threads and status indicators of the selected Actual Project Milestone Type.[IMAGE alt='' src='']Project threadsStatus indicators**DLOD Milestone type**EquipmentLogisticsInfrastructureOrganizationDoctrine/ConceptsInformationPersonnelTrainingDLOD AbsentNot RequiredNo Outstanding IssuesManageable IssuesCritical Issues[IMAGE alt='' src='']Project threadsStatus indicators**DOTMLPF Milestone type**DoctrineOrganizationTrainingMaterielLeadershipPersonnelFacilitiesMateriel Solution AnalysisTechnology DevelopmentEngineering and Manufacturing DevelopmentProduction & DeploymentOperations & SupportIf you selected your own milestone type, you can create your own projects threads and status indicators.To create project threads and status indicators1. In the Project Thread area, click the **Add** button.2. Specify the thread name.3. In the Status Indicator area, click the **Add** button.4. Specify the indicator name.5. Click the **Fill Color** button.6. In the **Color dialog** choose the color and click **OK**.To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread and click the **Clone**button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators is created.To remove the applied Project Status1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the right bottom corner.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Project Status to Lr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Project Status in PV-2 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing Project Status from Lr diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing Project Status from PV-2 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Project Status Application Wizard</strong> is used to apply the DLOD Milestone, DOTMLPF Milestone, or custom Milestone type to the selected element, which can be one of the following:</p><ul><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Deployed Milestone</li><li>No Longer Used Milestone</li><li>Actual Project Milestone</li></ul><p>To open the <strong>Project Status Application Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Milestone and select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.<br />The <strong>Project Status Application Wizard</strong> opens.</li></ol><p>The wizard consists of two steps:</p><ol><li><p>Select an Actual Project Milestone Type.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="3e1ab008-8412-4ee5-a597-9ece31ec1c64" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to select an Actual Project Milestone Type?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> In this step, select DLOD Milestone, DOTMLPF Milestone, or create your own Milestone type.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Selecting an Actual Project Milestone Type in Project Status Application Wizard" ac:title="Selecting an Actual Project Milestone Type in Project Status Application Wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_status_application_wizard_step1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Specify Project Status at a particular Milestone.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="5605d164-be6f-42fa-9ac7-eeebe3e49c0e" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify Project Status at a particular Milestone?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In this step, specify project threads and status indicators of the selected Actual Project Milestone Type.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying Project Status at particular Milestone in Project Status Application Wizard" ac:title="Specifying Project Status at particular Milestone in Project Status Application Wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_status_application_wizard_step2_DLOD.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><table><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>Project threads</th><th>Status indicators</th></tr><tr><td><strong>DLOD Milestone type</strong></td><td><ul><li>Equipment</li><li>Logistics</li><li>Infrastructure</li><li>Organization</li><li>Doctrine/Concepts</li><li>Information</li><li>Personnel</li><li>Training</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>DLOD Absent</li><li>Not Required</li><li>No Outstanding Issues</li><li>Manageable Issues</li><li>Critical Issues</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_status_application_wizard_step2_DOTMLPF.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><table><tbody><tr><th> </th><th>Project threads</th><th>Status indicators</th></tr><tr><td><strong>DOTMLPF Milestone type</strong></td><td><ul><li>Doctrine</li><li>Organization</li><li>Training</li><li>Materiel</li><li>Leadership</li><li>Personnel</li><li>Facilities</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Materiel Solution Analysis</li><li>Technology Development</li><li>Engineering and Manufacturing Development</li><li>Production &amp; Deployment</li><li>Operations &amp; Support</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p> </p><p>If you selected your own milestone type, you can create your own projects threads and status indicators.</p><p>To create project threads and status indicators</p><hr /><p>1. In the Project Thread area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</p><p>2. Specify the thread name.</p><p>3. In the Status Indicator area, click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</p><p>4. Specify the indicator name.</p><p>5. Click the <strong>Fill Color</strong> button.</p><p>6. In the <strong>Color dialog</strong> choose the color and click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="7b480ca1-6768-46d7-82dc-2938dc84c10f" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a second thread with the same status indicators, select a previously created thread and click the <strong>Clone </strong>button. A new thread with previously defined status indicators is created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To remove the applied Project Status</p><hr /><p>1. Click <ac:image ac:alt="Remove project status indicating mark" ac:title="Remove project status indicating mark"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the right bottom corner.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="fc61eb39-4c3b-4d73-9bc8-3b3e2d8e980a" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Applying Project Status to Lr diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Applying Project Status in PV-2 diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Removing Project Status from Lr diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Removing Project Status from PV-2 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: An aspect by which the progress of various Projects may be measured. In UK MOD, this could be one of the defense lines of development (DLOD), or DOTMLPF in the US.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, DoDAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Property

1339030544

##### Description

MODAF: A Project (MODAF::ProjectType, NAF::ProjectType) is used to define a category of project: For example, “Programme”, “Acquisition Project” or “Training Programme”.

DoDAF: NA (only Individual Project in DoDAF).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Class

## PAGE 00486: Project viewpoint

- page_id: `153989982`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153989982/Project+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:40:44.983+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['project-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1416628093

1416628095

1416628094

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Project Viewpoint describe how programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives deliver capabilities, the organizations contributing to them, and dependencies between them. Previous versions of DoDAF took a traditional model of architecture in which descriptions of programs and projects were considered outside scope. To compensate for this, various DoDAF models represented the evolution of systems, technologies and standards (e.g., Systems and Services Evolution Description, Systems Technology Forecast, and Technical Standards Forecast).” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

1416628092

**Related Pages:**

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1100880881

1100880883

1100880882

##### Description

UPDM: Asserts that a Resource type provides a competence.

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that a Role requires a Competence (MODAF::CompetenceForRole).

DoDAF: An overlap between a Personnel Type and the Skills it entails (DoDAF::SkillPartOfPersonType).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart)

[MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+SV-1+Resource+Interaction+Specification)

##### Related NAF views

[NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart)

[NSV-1 System Interface Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/NSV-1+System+Interface+Description)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="192fe302-d15c-4edf-ab65-d2c48cb5b320"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1100880881</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4631aa41-e13a-45e2-ac3f-2faa8102b8f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1100880883</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28e4c0c8-ac32-4829-823e-eb0987576ce7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1100880882</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span>Description</span></h3><p>UPDM: Asserts that a Resource type provides a competence.</p><p>MODAF/NAF: Asserts that a Role requires a Competence (MODAF::CompetenceForRole).</p><p>DoDAF: An overlap between a Personnel Type and the Skills it entails (DoDAF::SkillPartOfPersonType).</p><h3>Architecture Framework</h3><p>DoDAF, MODAF, NAF</p><h3>Extensions</h3><p><span>UML Dependency</span></p><h3>Related MODAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart">MODAF. OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart</a></p><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+SV-1+Resource+Interaction+Specification">MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification</a></p><h3>Related NAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/NOV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart">NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart</a></p><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/NSV-1+System+Interface+Description">NSV-1 System Interface Description</a></p><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990880 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00491: Publishing project as web portal

- page_id: `153990880`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990880/Publishing+project+as+web+portal
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-06-27T11:39:39.278+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Reports
- labels: ['interactive-web-portal', 'publish-project-as-web-portal']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Publish your project as interactive web portal and share it with stakeholders, including even those incapable of reading models in MagicDraw! The published web portal eliminates less needed elements from the model tree and proposes the most relevant views of enterprise architecture project, such as capability/strategic, operational, systems, services, standards, projects. This allows you to discover your project in a user-friendly and easily browsable environment.

If you want to publish a project as a web portal, you must install the Document Modeling Plugin. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing plugins' space='NMDOC']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can also provide feedback on any element or diagram while reviewing an Enterprise Architecture Portal report of the project.

Use quick search to find elements and diagrams for viewing their custom contents and share this information with colleagues.

Publishing the project as a web portal is as easy as generating any report.

To publish a project

1. On the main menu, click Tools > Report Wizard .
2. Select one of the following templates:
  - DoDAF > DoDAF Web Report
  - MODAF > MODAF Web Report
  - NAF > NAF Web Report
  - NAF 4 > NAF 4.0 Web Report
3. Follow the steps of the Report Wizard .
4. Place the output in a shared directory or on web server to make it available for co-workers.

The following table introduces what you can expect in your web report. In every view of the report, you may find the following information:

- What elements are collected
- Collected element properties
- Related elements
- Related diagram types

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153989983 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00492: PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships

- page_id: `153989983`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153989983/PV-1+Project+Portfolio+Relationships
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:19:33.301+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-1', 'pv-1-project-portfolio-relationships', 'pv-1-responsibility-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

29186712

29186714

29186713

**Description**

The PV-1 represents an organizational perspective on programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives.

The PV-1 enables the user to model the organizational structures needed to manage programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. It shows dependency relationships between the actual organizations that own the programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The PV-1 provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.

The intended usage of the PV-1 includes, but is not limited to:

- Program management (specified acquisition program structure).
- Project organization.
- Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.

**Implementation**

PV-1 can be represented using:

- A PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='PV-1_diagram.png']
- A PV-1 Responsibility Matrix which is an editable Dependency Matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='AcV-1_PV-1_matrix.PNG']

The model is strongly linked with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-4 Capability Dependencies' space='UPDM2P2024x'] model which shows capability groupings and dependencies.

29186711

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Organization' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Individual Person Role' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organizational Project Relationship' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Type' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Milestone' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Milestone Role' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone Role' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Status Indicators' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Activity' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity Part of Project' space='UPDM2P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="537e56c3-4745-4dcf-b7bb-13d01fa3fce3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29186712</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="513d6c28-a580-4742-9fc4-0e79320f3cda"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29186714</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6531cc8a-8c5e-4424-86ba-5fa5c69aee15"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29186713</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The PV-1 represents an organizational perspective on programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives.</p><p>The PV-1 enables the user to model the organizational structures needed to manage programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. It shows dependency relationships between the actual organizations that own the programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives. This model could be used to represent organizational relationships associated with transformation initiatives along with those who are responsible for managing programs, projects, and portfolios. The PV-1 provides a means of analyzing the main dependencies between acquisition elements or transformation elements.</p><p>The intended usage of the PV-1 includes, but is not limited to:</p><ul><li>Program management (specified acquisition program structure).</li><li>Project organization.</li><li>Cross-cutting initiatives to be tracked across portfolios.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>PV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships" ac:alt="PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships"><ri:attachment ri:filename="PV-1_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>A PV-1 Responsibility Matrix which is an editable Dependency Matrix.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="PV-1 Responsibility Matrix" ac:alt="PV-1 Responsibility Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="AcV-1_PV-1_matrix.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul><p>The model is strongly linked with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="CV-4 Capability Dependencies" /></ac:link> model which shows capability groupings and dependencies.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="322d6c29-5688-44dd-a388-7feb5aae4498"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">29186711</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7da87a64-8d47-496a-bd17-d496e4fcdc1a" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Actual Organization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Individual Person Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Organizational Project Relationship" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Project Type" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Project Milestone" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Project Milestone Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Status Indicators" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Project Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Activity Part of Project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity Part Of Project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153989988 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00493: PV-2 Project Timelines

- page_id: `153989988`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153989988/PV-2+Project+Timelines
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:07:27.631+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-2', 'pv-2-project-timelines', 'pv-2-gantt-chart']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

29712997

29712999

29712998

**Description**

The PV-2 provides a timeline perspective on programs. The PV-2 is intended primarily to support the acquisition and fielding processes including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of DoDD 5000.1 Defense Acquisition System policies to achieve a successfully integrated capability. The PV-2 is not limited to the acquisition and fielding processes.

The intended usage of the PV-2 includes:

- Project management and control (including delivery timescales).
- Project dependency risk identification.
- Management of dependencies.
- Portfolio management.

**Implementation**

PV-2 can be represented using a PV-2 diagram which is realized as a Gantt Chart.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Use of PV-2 should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-3 Capability Phasing' space='UPDM2P2024x'] model, if one exists.

29712996

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153989999 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00494: PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping

- page_id: `153989999`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153989999/PV-3+Project+to+Capability+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:09:01.998+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-3', 'pv-3-project-to-capability-mapping', 'pv-3-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

35598491

35598493

35598492

**Description**

The PV-3 supports the acquisition and deployment processes, including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all relevant project and program elements to achieve a capability.

The PV-3 maps programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives to capabilities to show how the specific elements help to achieve a capability. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives are mapped to the capability for a particular timeframe. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives may contribute to multiple capabilities and may mature across time. The analysis can be used to identify capability redundancies and shortfalls, highlight phasing issues, expose organizational or system interoperability problems, and support program decisions, such as when to phase out a legacy system.

The intended usage of the PV-3 includes:

- Tracing capability requirements to projects.
- Capability audit.

**Implementation**

PV-3 can be represented using a PV-3 diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Capabilities are used as row elements and Actual Projects are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This model is analogous to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024x'], but provides the interface between Capability and Project Models rather than Operational to System Models.

35598490

**Related Pages:**

****

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity Part of Capability' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity Part of Project' space='UPDM2P2024x']

Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives are mapped to the capability for a particular timeframe. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives may contribute to multiple capabilities and may mature across time. The analysis can be used to identify capability redundancies and shortfalls, highlight phasing issues, expose organizational or system interoperability problems, and support program decisions, such as when to phase out a legacy system.</p><p>The intended usage of the PV-3 includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing capability requirements to projects.</li><li>Capability audit.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>PV-3 can be represented using a PV-3 diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Capabilities are used as row elements and Actual Projects are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping" ac:alt="PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping"><ri:attachment ri:filename="PV-3.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>This model is analogous to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-5a Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, but provides the interface between Capability and Project Models rather than Operational to System Models.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d57e193e-b0ed-4ad1-93ad-1307256cae58"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">35598490</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2aa70e64-ec49-49fb-843d-7d0b9b529a0a" /></strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Activity Part of Capability" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity Part Of Capability]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Activity Part of Project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity Part Of Project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
