<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990860 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00495: Question dialog

- page_id: `153990860`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990860/Question+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-21T08:59:45.881+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Converting Model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks
- labels: ['question-dialog', 'convert-diagram-types', 'convert-element-types', 'convert-template-structure', 'display-project-conversion-confirmation-question']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Question** dialog opens when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Converting Model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks' space='UPDM2P2024x'].

The dialog asks whether user wants to convert the model according to specified options or not.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Check box | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Convert diagram types | Select to convert all diagram types to corresponding diagram types in the selected framework. |
| Convert element types | Select to convert all element types to their aliases in the selected framework. |
| Convert template structure | Select to convert the template structure to the template structure in the selected framework. |
| Do not show this message again | Select to hide the dialog the next time when performing the model conversion.To restore the usage of the Question dialogClick Options > Environment.In the Environment Options dialog select the UPDM options group.In the General properties, set the Display project conversion confirmation question property value to true. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Question</strong> dialog opens when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Converting Model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[converting a model to another Enterprise Architecture Framework]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>The dialog asks whether user wants to convert the model according to specified options or not.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Question dialog" ac:title="Question dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="question_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Question dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><table><tbody><tr><th>Check box</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Convert diagram types</td><td><p>Select to convert all diagram types to corresponding diagram types in the selected framework.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Convert element types</td><td><p>Select to convert all element types to their aliases in the selected framework.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Convert template structure</td><td><p>Select to convert the template structure to the template structure in the selected framework.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Do not show this message again</p></td><td><p>Select to hide the dialog the next time when performing the model conversion.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the <strong>Question</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UPDM </strong>options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Display project conversion confirmation question</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990804 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00496: Realized Operational Exchange wizard

- page_id: `153990804`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990804/Realized+Operational+Exchange+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T11:32:21.063+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['realized-operational-exchange-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Realized Operational Exchange** wizard gives the instructions to follow, when adding a new Operational Exchange or selecting one of the existing Operational Exchanges to:

- A Needline between a pair of Performer usages in OV-2 (NOV-2 in NAF) diagrams.
- An Operational Activity Edge between a pair of Operational Activities in OV-5 (NOV-5 in NAF) behavioral diagram.
- An Operational Message between a pair of two lifelines of Performers in OV-6c (NOV-6c in NAF) diagram.

The **Realized Operational Exchange** wizard includes of two steps:

1. Operational Exchange creation (mandatory). How to create a new or select an existing Operational Exchange?This step is designed to create a new Operational Exchange by specifying a Conveyed Item, both sending and receiving Performers, and a direction of a Operational Exchange. Depending on a fact, if selected Performers are known or not, requested information differs: 
- If sending and receiving Performers are unknown form the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']- If sending and receiving Performers are known, they are added to a **Realized Operational Exchange** wizard automatically. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']BoxDescriptionConveyed ItemOne or more Operational Exchange Items that are conveyed by the information flow from the sending Performers to receiving Performers.Operational ExchangeOperational Exchange that specifies an information flow for specified conveyed items.If you select an existing Operational Exchange from the drop-down list, Conveyed Item and Direction boxes will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit values of conveyed items.The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.Available if sending and receiving Performers are known from the wizard invocation context.Sending PerformerPerformer that sends the specified conveyed information.Available if a Performer is unknown from the wizard invocation context.Receiving PerformerPerformer that receives the specified conveyed information.Available if a Performer is unknown from the wizard invocation context.DirectionDirection of the information flow. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']After changing the direction from the default one, the sending Performance is switched with the receiving Performance. The direction of the Operational Exchange is changed accordingly.Add Operational Exchange to corresponding relationshipsSelect the check box, if you want to add the Operational Exchange to corresponding relationships existing in other related diagrams.ButtonDescription[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Conveyed Item** box)Click to open the **Select Conveyed Information** dialog to select or create a conveyed item. You can specify more than one conveyed item.[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Sending Performer**box)Click to open the element Selection dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Receiving****Performer**box)Click to open the element Selection dialog.
2. Producing and Consuming Activities specification (optional). How to specify Producing and Consuming Activities?This step is designed to specify Producing and Consuming Activities for the created or selected Operational Exchange. Operational Exchanges are produced and consumed by Activities acting under the Performers.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 Internal diagram
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-5b diagram
- Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-6c diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UPDM2P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard gives the instructions to follow, when adding a new Operational Exchange or selecting one of the existing Operational Exchanges to:</p><ul><li>A Needline between a pair of Performer usages in OV-2 (NOV-2 in NAF) diagrams.</li><li>An Operational Activity Edge between a pair of Operational Activities in OV-5 (NOV-5 in NAF) behavioral diagram.</li><li>An Operational Message between a pair of two lifelines of Performers in OV-6c (NOV-6c in NAF) diagram.</li></ul><p>The <strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard includes of two steps:</p><ol><li><p>Operational Exchange creation (mandatory).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="651cd12a-77f7-4e5d-82b6-54f2c5bdfeb9" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to create a new or select an existing Operational Exchange?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step is designed to create a new Operational Exchange by specifying a Conveyed Item, both sending and receiving Performers, and a direction of a Operational Exchange. Depending on a fact, if selected Performers are known or not, requested information differs:<br />- If sending and receiving Performers are unknown form the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="Creating new or selecting Operational Exchange in Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ac:title="Creating new or selecting Operational Exchange in Realized Operational Exchange wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_wizard_step1_1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>- If sending and receiving Performers are known, they are added to a <strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard automatically.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="Creating new or selecting Operational Exchange in Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ac:title="Creating new or selecting Operational Exchange in Realized Operational Exchange wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_wizard_step1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><table><tbody><tr><th>Box</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Conveyed Item</td><td><p>One or more Operational Exchange Items that are conveyed by the information flow from the sending Performers to receiving Performers.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Operational Exchange</td><td><p>Operational Exchange that specifies an information flow for specified conveyed items.</p><p>If you select an existing Operational Exchange from the drop-down list, Conveyed Item and Direction boxes will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit values of conveyed items.</p><p>The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="8d1d0bf6-f972-4359-894c-7aba5727d5bf" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if sending and receiving Performers are known from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></td></tr><tr><td>Sending Performer</td><td><p>Performer that sends the specified conveyed information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="1121be01-02da-4a03-9cfd-593c174eff13" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if a Performer is unknown from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></td></tr><tr><td>Receiving Performer</td><td><p> Performer that receives the specified conveyed information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="eeb42d77-7eb4-49a3-bb3b-0c44d2bd70f7" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if a Performer is unknown from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></td></tr><tr><td> Direction</td><td><p> Direction of the information flow.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying the direction" ac:title="Specifying the direction"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_wizard_direction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After changing the direction from the default one, the sending Performance is switched with the receiving Performance. The direction of the Operational Exchange is changed accordingly.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Add Operational Exchange to corresponding relationships</p></td><td><p>Select the check box, if you want to add the Operational Exchange to corresponding relationships existing in other related diagrams.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><table><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="[...]button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>(by the <strong>Conveyed Item</strong> box)</p></td><td><p>Click to open the <strong>Select Conveyed Information</strong> dialog to select or create a conveyed item. You can specify more than one conveyed item.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="[...]button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>(by the <strong>Sending Performer </strong>box)</p></td><td> Click to open the element Selection dialog.</td></tr><tr><td><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="[...]button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>(by the <strong>Receiving </strong><strong>Performer </strong>box)</p></td><td> Click to open the element Selection dialog.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Producing and Consuming Activities specification (optional).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="cb855e5b-1d49-429d-bd9a-fb7521c86700" ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to specify Producing and Consuming Activities?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step is designed to specify Producing and Consuming Activities for the created or selected Operational Exchange. Operational Exchanges are produced and consumed by Activities acting under the Performers.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Specifying Producing and Consuming Activities in Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ac:title="Specifying Producing and Consuming Activities in Realized Operational Exchange wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_wizard_step2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="8d4d74bb-45d8-4a40-8144-76474ed50c24" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><br /><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Operational Exchanges in L2 Logical Scenario diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDMP/Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+OV-2+diagram">Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram</a></li><li><a <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990809 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00497: Realized Resource Interaction wizard

- page_id: `153990809`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990809/Realized+Resource+Interaction+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T11:45:12.583+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['realized-resource-interaction-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Realized Resource Interaction** wizard provides you with the instructions to follow when adding a new Resource Interaction to:

- A Resource Interface between a pair of Resource Roles in the SV-1, SV-2 diagrams.
- A Function Edge (Function Control Flow and Function Object Flow) between a pair of Functions in the SV-4 Flows diagram.
- A Resource Message between a pair of two lifelines of Resources in the SV-10c diagram.

The **Realized Resource Interaction** wizard includes of two steps:

1. Resource Interaction creation (mandatory). How to create new or select an existing Resource Interaction?This step is designed to create a new Resource Interaction by specifying a conveyed Resource Interaction Item, both sending and receiving Resources, and a direction of a Resource Interaction. Depending on a fact, if selected Resources are known or not, requested information differs: 
- If sending and receiving Resources are unknown form the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']- If sending and receiving Resources are known, they are added to a **Realized Resource Interaction** wizard automatically. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']BoxDescriptionConveyed ItemOne or more Resource Interaction Items that are conveyed by the information flow from the sending Resource to receiving Resource.Resource InteractionResource Interaction that specifies an information flow for specified conveyed items.If you select an existing Resource Interaction from the drop-down list, Conveyed Item and Direction boxes will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit values of conveyed items.The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.Available if sending and receiving Resources are known from the wizard invocation context.Sending ResourceResource that sends the specified conveyed information.Available if a Resource is unknown from the wizard invocation context.Receiving ResourceResource that receives the specified conveyed information.Available if a Resource is unknown from the wizard invocation context.DirectionDirection of the information flow. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']After changing the direction from the default one, the sending resource will be switched with the receiving resource. The direction of the Resource Interaction will be changed accordingly.Add Resource Interaction to corresponding relationshipsSelect the check box, if you want to add the Resource Interaction to corresponding relationships existing in other related diagrams. These are the relationships that can realize the Resource Interaction:- System Connector (SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description, SV-2 Systems Communications Internal Description)- Resource Connector (DoDAF) / Resource Interface (MODAF / NAF) (SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description, SV-2 Systems Communications Internal Description)- Function Edge (SV-4 Systems Functionality Description)- Resource Message (SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description)ButtonDescription[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Conveyed Item** box)Click to open the element Selection dialog to select or create a conveyed item. You can specify more than one conveyed item.[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Sending Resource** box)Click to open the element Selection dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src=''](by the **Receiving Resource** box)Click to open the element Selection dialog.
2. step2Producing and Consuming Functions specification (optional). Click here for detailed descriptionThis step is designed to specify Producing and Consuming Functions for the created or selected Resource Interaction. Resource Interactions are produced and consumed by Activities acting under the Resource (System Function (DoDAF) or Function (MODAF / NAF)). In terms of UPDM, Resources perform System Functions (DoDAF) or Functions (MODAF / NAF).[IMAGE alt='' src='']There should always be a pair of functions: one to produce and other to consume information flow

To open the **Realized Resource Interaction** wizard do one of the following

- Create the Resource Interaction relationship between System Resources. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']**MODAF:**SV-1 Resource Interaction SpecificationSV-2 Resource Communications DescriptionSV-4 Functionality Description**NAF:**NSV-1 System Interface DescriptionNSV-2 System Communications DescriptionNSV-4 System Functionality Description
- Select a Resource Connector or a Resource Interface and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the smart manipulator toolbar. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']**MODAF:**SV-1 Resource Internal Interaction SpecificationSV-2 Resource Internal Communications Description
- On the diagram pallet, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select a Resource Connector or a Resource Interface. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']**MODAF:**SV-1 Resource Internal Interaction SpecificationSV-2 Resource Internal Communications Description
- Select a Function Edge and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the smart manipulator toolbar. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']**MODAF:**SV-4 Functionality Description**NAF:**NSV-4 System Functionality Description
- In the diagram pallet, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select a Function Edge. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']**MODAF:**SV-4 Functionality Description**NAF:**NSV-4 System Functionality Description
- On the table toolbar, click the**Add New** button. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']**MODAF:**SV-4 Functionality Description**NAF:**NSV-4 System Functionality Description
- Select a Resource Message and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the smart manipulator toolbar. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']**MODAF:**SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description**NAF:**NSV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description
- In the diagram pallet, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select a Resource Message. Click here to see the views for which this action is available**DoDAF:**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']**MODAF:**SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description**NAF:**NSV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990467 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00498: Relating Projects in Lr diagram

- page_id: `153990467`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990467/Relating+Projects+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-31T09:36:09.050+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)
- labels: ['relate-project-in-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.

You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate projects

1. Select two Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select one of the available relation types:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between projects

1. Select two related Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None .

To resolve the incorrect project sequence

1. On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click Validate .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you change dates of a Project containing Sub Projects, the Sub Project dates change correspondingly.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:</p><ul><li>Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.</li><li>Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.</li></ul><p>You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.</p><p> </p><p>To relate projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the available relation types:<br /><ul><li><strong>Finish to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Finish to Finish</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Finish</strong></li></ul></li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation between projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two related Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>None</strong>.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To resolve the incorrect project sequence</p><hr /><ol><li>On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click <strong>Validate</strong>.</li><li><p>Select one of the suggested solutions.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="PV-2_Validation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Relating Projects in Lr diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="1fb35907-6f21-414e-9f69-ae4a3c73a422" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change dates of a Project containing Sub Projects, the Sub Project dates change correspondingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990418 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00500: Removing Capabilities from Cr diagram

- page_id: `153990418`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990418/Removing+Capabilities+from+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T16:24:19.022+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['delete-capabilities-from-chart-in-cr', 'remove-capability-from-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To delete a Capability from the chart

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

To delete a Capability from the model

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To delete a Capability from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> </strong> <strong>Delete From Chart </strong>button.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To delete a Capability from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> </strong> <strong>Delete</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

- page_id: `153990469`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990469/Removing+Project+Status+from+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-31T09:33:32.864+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart)
- labels: ['remove-project-status-from-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove the Project Status

1. From the Milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. In the right bottom corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UPDM2P2024x'] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To remove the Project Status</p><hr /><ol><li>From the Milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.</li><li>In the right bottom corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:alt="Remove project status indicating mark" ac:title="Remove project status indicating mark"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Physical Resource viewpoint > Pr Configuration Management
- labels: ['remove-resource-in-pr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove a Resource

1. Right-click the Resource bar and from the shortcut menu, select Remove Resource > <name of the resource you want to remove>.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To remove a Resource</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Resource bar and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Remove Resource</strong> &gt; &lt;name of the resource you want to remove&gt;.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

- page_id: `153990419`

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990419/Removing+Sub+Capabilities+from+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-07T16:23:37.372+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Concepts viewpoint > Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['delete-sub-capabilities-from-chart-in-cr', 'remove-sub-capabilities-from-cr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To delete a Sub Capability from the chart

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

To delete a Sub Capability from the model

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To delete a Sub Capability from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Sub Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> Delete From Chart</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To delete a Sub Capability from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Sub Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> </strong> <strong>Delete</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

1106364366

1106364368

1106364367

##### Description

A RequestPoint models the use of a service by a participant and defines the connection point through which a Participant makes requests and uses or consumes services.

##### Origin

SoaML

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Port

##### Related DoDAF views

[SV-1 Systems Interface Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description)

## PAGE 00509: Requires Competence

- page_id: `153990690`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990690/Requires+Competence
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:32:23.206+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1100968495

1100968497

1100968496

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that an Role requires a Competence (MODAF::CompetenceForRole, NAF::CompetenceForRole).

DoDAF: An overlap between a Personnel Type and the Skills it entails (DoDAF::SkillPartOfPersonType).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Dependency

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

315044503

315044505

315044504

##### Description

UPDM: A combination of physical element, energy, and data that are combined used to accomplish a task or function.

MODAF/NAF: A type of man-made object. Examples are “car”, “radio”, “fuel”, etc. (MODAF::Artifact, NAF::Artifact).

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| facilityType : String[0..*] | The type of facility that the artifact is associated with. |

##### Extensions

UML Class

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

506464327

506464329

506464328

##### Description

UPDM: A physical connection between two resources that implements protocols through which the source resource can transmit items to the destination resource.

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that a connection exists between two ports belonging to parts in a system composite structure model (MODAF::SystemPortConnector, NAF::SystemPortConnector).

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Connector

##### Related MODAF view

[MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+SV-1+Resource+Interaction+Specification)

[MODAF. SV-2 Resource Internal Communications Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+SV-2+Resource+Internal+Communications+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NSV-1 System Interface Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/NSV-1+System+Interface+Description)

[NSV-2 System Communications Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/NSV-2+System+Communications+Description)

554687045

554687047

554687046

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A rule governing the structural or functional aspects of an implementation - this may also include constraints on OrganizationalResources that are part of an implementation.

DoDAF: The range of permissible states for an object (DoDAF::Constraint).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| InformationTechnologyStandardCategory : String[*] | The information technology standard category that is associated with the resource constraint. |

##### Extensions

UML Constraint

##### Related DoDAF views

[SV-10a Systems Rules Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/SV-10a+Systems+Rules+Model)

##### Related MODAF views

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

554790083

554790085

554790084

##### Description

UPDM: ResourceInteraction represents data that is exchanged between the resources.

MODAF/NAF: An assertion that two FunctionalResources interact. For example, data exchange between systems, conversations between people, people using systems.

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Information Flow

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990814 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00515: Resource Interaction Manager dialog

- page_id: `153990814`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990814/Resource+Interaction+Manager+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-08-10T11:46:39.403+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['resource-interaction-manager-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Resource Interaction Manager dialog enables to analyze and manage the Resource Interactions data. You can edit existing Resource Interactions by adding new Resource Interaction Items, realizing or ignoring existing Resource Interactions on selected relationship, specifying producing and consuming Functions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Resource Interaction Manager** dialog

1. Select Resource Interface, Resource Connector, Association, Function Edge , or Resource Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_interaction_manager.png'] . The Resource Interaction Manager dialog opens.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Interaction in P1 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Interaction in P2 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P3 table' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Interaction in P4 Resource Functions diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Interaction in P6 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-1 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-2 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-4 Service Functionality Description
- Creating Resource Interaction in SvcV-10c diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-1 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-1 Internal diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 Internal diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram
- Creating Resource Interaction in SV-10c diagram
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Interaction wizard' space='UPDM2P2024x']

## PAGE 00516: Resource Interface

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

554850866

554850868

554850867

##### Description

UPDM: ResourceInterface is a contractual agreement between two resources that implement protocols through which the source resource to the destination resource.

MODAF/NAF: NA

DoDAF: An overlap between Performers for the purpose of producing a Resource that is consumed by the other (DoDAF::Interface).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Association

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+SV-1+Resource+Interaction+Specification)

## PAGE 00517: Resource Message

- page_id: `153990733`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

554913424

554913426

554913425

##### Description

UPDM: Message for use in a Resource Event-Trace, implements a ResourceInteraction.

MODAF/NAF: A specification of the interactions between aspects of a Resources architecture (MODAF::ResourceInteractionSpecification, NAF::ResourceInteractionSpecification).

DoDAF: An overlap of an Activity with a Resource, in particular a consuming or producing Activity that expresses an input, output, consumption, or production Activity of the Resource (DoDAF::ActivityResourceOverlap).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Message

##### Related DoDAF views

[SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/SV-10c+Systems+Event-Trace+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+SV-10c+Resource+Event-Trace+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NSV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/NSV-10c+Systems+Event-Trace+Description)

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

555062006

555062008

555062007

##### Description

UPDM: Port is an interaction point for a resource through which it can interact with the outside environment.

MODAF/NAF: An interface (logical or physical) provided by a System. A SystemPort may implement a PortType though there is no requirement for SystemPorts to be typed (MODAF::SystemPort, NAF::SystemPort).

DoDAF: An interface (logical or physical) provided by a System (DoDAF::Port).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Port

##### Related DoDAF views

[SV-1 Systems Interface Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description)

[SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/SV-2+Systems+Internal+Resource+Flow+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-2 Resource Internal Communications Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+SV-2+Resource+Internal+Communications+Description)

##### Related NAF views

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990897 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00525: Reusing Measurement Sets for Actual Objects

- page_id: `153990897`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990897/Reusing+Measurement+Sets+for+Actual+Objects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-25T08:31:34.672+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['reuse-measurement-sets-for-actual-objects']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a Node Role or Resource Role, you can quickly reuse the Measurement Sets of their type (Node or System Resource). For your convenience, the information about the recommended Actual Measurement Sets is displayed in the Specification window of an element for which you are creating the Actual Measurement Set.

You can conveniently reuse the values of Measurement Sets when creating the following actual objects:

- Node Role
- Resource Role
- Operational Activity Action
- Function Action
- Instances

When creating instances, the Actual Measurement Sets belonging to the classifier are copied automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The type and behavior measurements in the Actual Object Measurements compartment are displayed *Italic*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

- page_id: `153990693`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990693/Rule
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:27:31.864+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Operational Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1101034189

1101034191

1101034190

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: An abstract Class that is extended by OperationalConstraint (A rule governing an operational behaviour or property.) and ResourceConstraint (A rule governing the structural or functional aspects of an implementation - this may also include constraints on OrganisationalResources that are part of an implementation).

DoDAF: Rule: A principle or condition that governs behavior; a prescribed guide for conduct or action. Subtype: Constraint: The range of permissible states for an object.

Rule is abstract.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Constraint

##### Related DoDAF views

[OV-6a Operational Rules Model](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/OV-6a+Operational+Rules+Model)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990471 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00528: S1 Service Taxonomy

- page_id: `153990471`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990471/S1+Service+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T10:57:56.106+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s1-service-taxonomy', 's1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The S1 Service Taxonomy view specifies a hierarchy of services. The elements in the hierarchy are service specifications (rather than service implementations), and the relationships between the elements are specializations – i.e. one service is a special type of another. The purpose of an S1 is to provide a governance structure for a Service-Oriented Architecture. Along with S3-Service Interfaces, it specifies a standard library of service specifications for an enterprise, which service implementers are expected to conform to.

A service is an implementation-independent specification of a packaged element of functionality and/or capability. There is potential for confusion between services and capabilities. The key indicator of a service is that it provides a standard interface to consumers. This means that services may be used as “wrappers” for one or more capability in order to provide a standard method of access to the capability. A well-designed service taxonomy provides a set of specifications for capability providers to adhere to. Services may have attributes and constraints (service policy) defined against them. Attributes are inherited by specialized services. Where an attribute is specified for a service, implementations of that service shall specify their values for the attribute.

The S1 view may be used to:

- SOA governance.
- Identification of services.
- Service planning.
- Service audit.
- Service gap analysis.
- Providing reference services for architectures.
- Tailoring generic services for specific applications.

**Implementation**

The S1 view can be represented using:

- A S1 Service Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S1.png']
- A C1-S1 Capability to Services Mapping matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='S1_matrix.png']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S1 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C1-S1 matrix' space='UPDM2P2024x']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interface
- Capability
- Expose

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The S1 Service Taxonomy view specifies a hierarchy of services. The elements in the hierarchy are service specifications (rather than service implementations), and the relationships between the elements are specializations – i.e. one service is a special type of another. The purpose of an S1 is to provide a governance structure for a Service-Oriented Architecture. Along with S3-Service Interfaces, it specifies a standard library of service specifications for an enterprise, which service implementers are expected to conform to.</p><p><span>A service is an implementation-independent specification of a packaged element of functionality and/or capability. There is potential for confusion between services and capabilities. The key indicator of a service is that it provides a standard interface to consumers. This means that services may be used as “wrappers” for one or more capability in order to provide a standard method of access to the capability. A well-designed service taxonomy provides a set of specifications for capability providers to adhere to. Services may have attributes and constraints (service policy) defined against them. Attributes are inherited by specialized services. Where an attribute is specified for a service, implementations of that service shall specify their values for the attribute.</span></p><p>The S1 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>SOA governance.</li><li>Identification of services.</li><li>Service planning.</li><li>Service audit.</li><li>Service gap analysis.</li><li>Providing reference services for architectures.</li><li>Tailoring generic services for specific applications.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S1 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A S1 Service Taxonomy diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:alt=" S1 Service Taxonomy diagram" ac:title=" S1 Service Taxonomy diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="S1 Service Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>A C1-S1 Capability to Services Mapping matrix.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="S1 Capability to Services Mapping matrix" ac:title="S1 Capability to Services Mapping matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S1_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="S1 Service Taxonomy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating S1 diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating C1-S1 matrix" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Interface</li><li>Capability</li><li>Expose</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990476 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00529: S3 Service Interfaces

- page_id: `153990476`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990476/S3+Service+Interfaces
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T11:01:02.194+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s3-service-interfaces', 's3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of the S3 Service Interfaces view is to define the interfaces presented by a service. A service presents one or more interfaces to consumers (a “consumer” being any agent capable of using the service – a person, an organization, a system or another service). In this case, the architect specifies provided interfaces. A service may also be capable of using interfaces exposed by other services, and the architect may specify these as used interfaces.

Specifying the interfaces that a service provides and uses defines which services are compatible with which other services. If Service A provides an interface, X and Service B can use Interface X then Service B can call upon at least some of the functionality of Service A.

**Implementation**

The S3 view can be represented using:

- A S3 Service Interfaces diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S3.png']
- A S3 MS Word document report.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interface
- Service Operation
- Service Attribute
- Service Parameter
- Service
- Request

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

A service presents one or more interfaces to consumers (a “consumer” being any agent capable of using the service – a person, an organization, a system or another service). In this case, the architect specifies provided interfaces. A service may also be capable of using interfaces exposed by other services, and the architect may specify these as used interfaces.</p><p>Specifying the interfaces that a service provides and uses defines which services are compatible with which other services. If Service A provides an interface, X and Service B can use Interface X then Service B can call upon at least some of the functionality of Service A.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S3 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A S3 Service Interfaces diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="S3 Service Interfaces diagram" ac:title="S3 Service Interfaces diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="S3 Service Interfaces" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="114de996-cdfe-4074-886b-5de4d3ff8db8">A S3 MS Word document report.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Interface</li><li>Service Operation</li><li>Service Attribute</li><li>Service Parameter</li><li>Service</li><li>Request</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990478 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00530: S4 Service Functions

- page_id: `153990478`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990478/S4+Service+Functions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T11:01:39.666+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s4-service-functions', 's4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The S4 Service Functions view defines the behavior of a service in terms of the functions it is expected to perform. S4 is the key behavioral specification for services. Equivalent in nature to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UPDM2P2024x'] and R4 - Resource Functions, it specifies a set of functions that a service implementation is expected to perform.

**Implementation**

The S4 view can be represented using:

- A S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram based on the UML Clas diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S4.png']

- A S4 Service Functions diagram for the Operational Activity flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='S4_service_functions.png']
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S4 Service Functions diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Function
- Service Operation Action
- Service Function Action
- Function Edge
- Service Operation
- Service Parameter
- Service Interface

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990483 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00531: S5 Service States

- page_id: `153990483`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990483/S5+Service+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T11:02:20.489+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s5-service-states', 's5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of the S5 Service States view is to specify the possible states a service may have, and the possible transitions between those states. It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. S5 is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.

**Implementation**

The S5 view can be represented using a S5 Service States diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service State Machine
- Service Interface

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave. S5 is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S5 view can be represented using a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ee4b8256-c894-4fc6-aa98-222af27fda31">S5 Service States diagram</ac:inline-comment-marker>.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="S5 Service States diagram" ac:title="S5 Service States diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S5.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="S5 Service States" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service State Machine</li><li>Service Interface</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990485 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00532: S6 Service Interactions

- page_id: `153990485`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990485/S6+Service+Interactions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T11:02:42.813+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s6-service-interactions', 's6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of S6 Service Interactions view is to specify how a service interacts with external agents, and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions. An S6 product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L6 Logical Sequence' space='UPDM2P2024x']). Its purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.

**Implementation**

The S6 view can be represented using a S6 Service Interactions diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interaction
- Service Message
- Service Interface
- Service Attribute

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

An S6 product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="L6 Logical Sequence" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L6 - Logical Sequence]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). Its purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S6 view can be represented using a S6 Service Interactions diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="S6 Service Interactions diagram" ac:title="S6 Service Interactions diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S6.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="S6 Service Interactions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Interaction</li><li>Service Message</li><li>Service Interface</li><li>Service Attribute</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The S7 Service I/F Parameters view identifies all the parameters used in Service Interfaces (see also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='S3 Service Interfaces' space='UPDM2P2024x']).

**Implementation**

The S7 view can be represented using S7 Service I/F Parameters diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Interface
- Service Operation
- Service Attribute
- Service Parameter
- Service
- Request

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990489 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00534: S8 Service Policy

- page_id: `153990489`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990489/S8+Service+Policy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-05T11:03:53.033+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['s8-service-policy', 's8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The purpose of the S8 Service Policy view is to specify constraints that apply to implementations of services. To better enable consistency and re-use of service specifications, it is important to set constraints on how a service should behave. An S8 product specifies constraints against services to which implementations of must conform.

**Implementation**

The S8 view can be represented using:

- A S8 Service Policy table. [ATTACHMENT filename='S8.png']
- A S8 spreadsheet report .

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S8 table' space='UPDM2P2024x']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Policy
- Service Interface

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

An S8 product specifies constraints against services to which implementations of must conform.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The S8 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A S8 Service Policy table.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="S8 Service Policy table" ac:title="S8 Service Policy table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S8.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="S8 Service Policy" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6a3a4163-27db-4f78-a534-afc9df3f4eb6">A S8 spreadsheet report</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating S8 table" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Policy</li><li>Service Interface</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990850 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00538: Select Symbol dialog

- page_id: `153990850`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990850/Select+Symbol+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-10-20T13:29:23.968+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Dialogs
- labels: ['select-symbol-dialog', 'affiliation', 'frame']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use **Select Symbol** dialog to apply military symbols for resources.

To open the **Select Symbol** dialog, do either

- Create resources from MIL-STD-2525C Symbology toolbar.
- Select resource and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] on the smart manipulator toolbar.
- Open the Specification window of any UPDM element. Select Image property cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] . The **Select Symbol** dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Select Symbol** dialog consists of these four components:

##### Whole hierarchy tree/ Recently used tree

Select the military symbol from the Whole hierarchy tree or Recently used tree.

##### Quick filter

You can search for symbols in the **Whole hierarchy** or **Recently used** tabs by using one of the following way:

- Simple Search: type what you want to find in the hierarchy.
- ID Search: use an MIL-STD-2525C symbology ID to find the exact symbol in the hierarchy. For example, type "1.X.3.2.2.1.3".
- Pattern Search: type * to find any symbols. For example, type "*Frying" if you want to find "Fixed Wing" somewhere in the hierarchy.

##### Affiliation area

Affiliation - the threat posed by the warfighting object being represented. The basic affiliation categories are unknown, friend, neutral, and hostile. Additionally the pending, suspect, assumed friend, joker and faker affiliations may be applied. Affiliation can be defined for all military symbols available. Its a bit different with a frame property. It can be changed for no more than 1/5 of a symbols.

##### Frame area

Frame - the geometric border of a symbol that provides an indication of the affiliation, battle dimension, and status of a warfighting object.

##### Preview area

You can preview the symbol before applying it on the resource.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying military symbols' space='UPDM2P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Select <strong>Image</strong> property cell and click <ac:image ac:alt="MIL-STD-2525C Symbology" ac:title="MIL-STD-2525C Symbology"><ri:attachment ri:filename="military_symbols.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br />The <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Select Symbol</strong> dialog</span> opens.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:alt="Select Symbol dialog" ac:title="Select Symbol dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_symbol_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Select Symbol dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The <strong>Select Symbol</strong> dialog consists of these four components:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="6f3a7da5-9780-4043-9b1d-1479391dbc99" ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" /></p><h3>Whole hierarchy tree/ Recently used tree</h3><p>Select the military symbol from the Whole hierarchy tree or Recently used tree.</p><h3>Quick filter</h3><p>You can search for symbols in the <strong>Whole hierarchy</strong> or <strong>Recently used</strong> tabs by using one of the following way:<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Simple Search: type what you want to find in the hierarchy. </span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">ID Search: use an MIL-STD-2525C symbology ID to find the exact symbol in the hierarchy.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="35bee693-e8a4-4b4e-8c4f-2bcc8ed0b2c3" ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, type &quot;1.X.3.2.2.1.3&quot;.</span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Pattern Search: type * to find any symbols.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="660312ee-045d-4875-a612-1aa2773946ac" ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, type &quot;*Frying&quot; if you want to find &quot;Fixed Wing&quot; somewhere in the hierarchy.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Affiliation area</span><br /></span></h3><p>Affiliation - the threat posed by the warfighting object being represented. The basic affiliation categories are unknown, friend, neutral, and hostile. Additionally the pending, suspect, assumed friend, joker and faker affiliations may be applied. Affiliation can be defined for all military symbols available. Its a bit different with a frame property. It can be changed for no more than 1/5 of a symbols.</p><h3>Frame area</h3><p>Frame - the geometric border of a symbol that provides an indication of the affiliation, battle dimension, and status of a warfighting object.</p><h3>Preview area</h3><p>You can preview the symbol before applying it on the resource.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="1b223146-dd7b-4b24-b2a5-1bd0d50f609e" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Applying military symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Applying military symbols ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

314459507

314459509

314459508

##### Description

A ServicePoint is the offer of a service by one participant to others using well defined terms, conditions and interfaces. A ServicePoint defines the connection point through which a Participant offers its capabilities and provides a service to clients.

##### Origin

SoaML

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Port

##### Related DoDAF views

[SV-1 Systems Interface Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description)

[SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/SvcV-2+Services+Resource+Flow+Description)

[SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/SvcV-3a+Systems-Services+Matrix)

[SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/SvcV-6+Services+Resource+Flow+Matrix)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990956 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00543: Service Channels Summary Matrix

- page_id: `153990956`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990956/Service+Channels+Summary+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T12:08:05.798+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['service-channels-summary-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Service Channel Summary matrix enables a quick overview of all the service interactions specified in one or more OV and SV models. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies). In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services.

The Service Channel Summary Matrix can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of service pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose. One type of organization is a Service Hierarchy or Taxonomy of Services.

The intended usage of the Service Channel Summary Matrix includes:

- Summarizing service resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

Service Channel Summary Matrix can be represented using a Service Channel Summary Matrix diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Channel
- Service Interface
- Service
- Request
- System
- Materiel
- Post

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies). In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services.</p><p>The Service Channel Summary Matrix can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of service pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose. One type of organization is a Service Hierarchy or Taxonomy of Services.</p><p>The intended usage of the Service Channel Summary Matrix includes:</p><ul><li>Summarizing service resource interactions.</li><li>Interface management.</li><li>Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>Service Channel Summary Matrix can be represented using a Service Channel Summary Matrix diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:alt="Service Channels Summary Matrix" ac:title="Service Channels Summary Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_channel_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Service Channels Summary Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="1f0b3eb9-36a5-41e5-a72b-e77ab6d3906e" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="3a3bce9f-9b30-427a-bde5-c2ae7cb4f43c" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="a6ca68c0-0cec-427b-8490-b1bd425587f7" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Channel</li><li>Service Interface</li><li>Service</li><li>Request</li><li>System</li><li>Materiel</li><li>Post</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990953 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00544: Service Channels Summary Table

- page_id: `153990953`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990953/Service+Channels+Summary+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T12:08:26.513+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures
- labels: ['service-channels-summary-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Service Channel Summary Table specifies the characteristics of service resource flows between Services. The Service Channel Summary Table is the physical equivalent of the logical OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix and provides detailed information on the service connections which implement the Resource Flow exchanges specified in OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix.

In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. According to the Net-Centric Data Strategy, a net-centric implementation needs to focus in on the data in the Service Resource Flow, as well as the services that produce or consume the data of the Service Resource Flow. In a net-centric implementation, not all the consumers are known and this model emphasizes the focus on the producer and Service Resource Flow.

**Implementation**

Service Channel Summary Table can be represented using:

- A Service Channel Summary Table diagram.
- A Service Channel Summary Table spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Service Channel
- Service Interface
- Service
- Request
- Expose
- Service Operation
- Service Parameter
- Performer
- System
- Materiel
- Capability Configuration
- Post

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

According to the Net-Centric Data Strategy, a net-centric implementation needs to focus in on the data in the Service Resource Flow, as well as the services that produce or consume the data of the Service Resource Flow. In a net-centric implementation, not all the consumers are known and this model emphasizes the focus on the producer and Service Resource Flow.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>Service Channel Summary Table can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A Service Channel Summary Table diagram.</li><li>A Service Channel Summary Table spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Service Channels Summary Table" ac:title="Service Channels Summary Table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_channel_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Service Channels Summary Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="a616e52b-7e4f-413a-ab29-a62e36a01c0e" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="af26e872-bc7d-43d6-b803-527eef26635c" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="74e7d035-d63a-4378-a628-95a49e07a728" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Channel</li><li>Service Interface</li><li>Service</li><li>Request</li><li>Expose</li><li>Service Operation</li><li>Service Parameter</li><li>Performer</li><li>System</li><li>Materiel</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Post</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

244656554

244656557

244656555

##### Description

UPDM: Package containing the elements that describe a service, from DoDAF 2.

DoDAF: Information necessary to interact with the service in such terms as the service inputs, outputs, and associated semantics. The service description also conveys what is accomplished when the service is invoked and the conditions for using the service.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF

##### Extensions

UML Package

232471218

##### Description

UPDM: A ServiceFunction describes the abstract behavior of ServiceOperations, regardless of the actual implementation.

MODAF/NAF: A type of activity describing the functionality of a service.

DoDAF: Information necessary to interact with the service in such terms as the service inputs, outputs, and associated semantics. The service description also conveys what is accomplished when the service is invoked and the conditions for using the service.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Activity
