The service description also conveys what is accomplished when the service is invoked and the conditions for using the service.</span></p><h3><span>Architecture Framework</span></h3><p><span>DoDAF, MODAF, NAF</span></p><h3><span>Extensions</span></h3><p><span>UML Activity</span></p><h3>Related DoDAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description">SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description</a></p><h3>Related MODAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Flow">MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow</a></p><h3>Related NAF views</h3><p><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/NSOV-5+Service+Behaviour+Definition">NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition</a></p><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00547: Service Function Action

- page_id: `153990704`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990704/Service+Function+Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:02:01.069+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Services Viewpoint and Service Oriented Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

244523906

244523910

244523909

##### Description

UPDM: A call behavior action that invokes the ServiceFunction that needs to be preformed. This concept is required for mapping the architecture with UML and does not have a DoDAF or MODAF equivalent.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Call Behavior Action

##### Related DoDAF views

[SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Flow)

##### Related NAF views

[NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/NSOV-5+Service+Behaviour+Definition)

245535464

UPDM: Interaction for a service interface.

MODAF/NAF: A model representing how a set of Service classes interacts with one another (MODAF::ServiceInteractionSpecification, NAF::ServiceInteractionSpecification).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Interaction

## PAGE 00549: Service Interface

- page_id: `153990707`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990707/Service+Interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:15:40.976+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Services Viewpoint and Service Oriented Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

246531099

246531102

246531101

##### Description

UPDM: A contractual agreement between two resources that implement protocols through which the source service interacts to the destination resource. A physical connection between two resources that implements protocols through which the source resource can transmit items to the destination resource.

MODAF/NAF: The mechanism by which a Service communicates.

DoDAF: An overlap between Performers for the purpose of producing a Resource that is consumed by the other (DoDAF::Interface).

SOAML: Defines the interface to a Service Point or Request Point and is the type of a role in a service contract.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| serviceInteraction : ServiceInteraction[0..1] | A Service interaction. |
| serviceStateMachine : ServiceStateMachine[0..1] | A Service state machine. |

##### Extensions

UML Class

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

| carries : ResourceInteraction[*] | A Carried ResourceInteraction. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990709 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00551: Service Operation

- page_id: `153990709`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990709/Service+Operation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:11:26.101+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Services Viewpoint and Service Oriented Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

249474688

249474690

249474689

##### Description

UPDM: A ServiceOperation provides the access point for invoking the behavior of a provided service. The ServiceOperations are defined on ServiceInterfaces and mirrored on the providing Resource to handle calls forwarded on by the interface.

MODAF/NAF: A function or procedure which enables programmatic communication with a Service via a ServiceInterface (MODAF::ServiceInterfaceOperation).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| abstractBehavior : ServiceFunction[0..1] | Links a ServiceOperation to the abstract description of its behavior, as provided by a ServiceFunction. |
| concreteBehavior : Function[0..1] | Links a ServiceOperation to the concrete description of its behavior, as provided by a Function. |

##### Extensions

UML Operation

## PAGE 00552: Service Operation Action

- page_id: `153990710`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

249789275

249789278

249789277

##### Description

UPDM Artifact: A call action that represents a Resource or Service Function invoking a ServiceOperation. This is used by a consuming Resource to model the call into the service. This concept is required for mapping the architecture with UML and does not have a DoDAF or MODAF equivalent.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Call Operation Action

##### Related DoDAF views

[SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description)

##### Related NAF views

[NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/NSOV-5+Service+Behaviour+Definition)

DoDAF: NA

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990470 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00556: Service viewpoint

- page_id: `153990470`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990470/Service+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-05-13T10:11:38.444+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['service-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Service viewpoint is based on the concept of a Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA), which is fundamental to the NNEC paradigm. It is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logical viewpoint' space='UPDM2P2024x']. A service, within the Service viewpoint, is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer. It focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The viewpoint also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behavior.

#### PANEL: Views

Views

1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A service, within the Service viewpoint, is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer. It focuses strictly on identifying and describing services. The viewpoint also supports the description of service taxonomies, service orchestrations, a mapping of services to operational activities, and a description of various forms of service behavior.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990047 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00557: Services viewpoint

- page_id: `153990047`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990047/Services+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:42:26.233+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['services-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

984167070

984167073

984167072

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Services Viewpoint describes services and their interconnections providing or supporting, DoD functions. DoD functions include both warfighting and business functions. The Service Models associate service resources to the operational and capability requirements. These resources support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information. The relationship between architectural data elements across the Services Viewpoint to the Operational Viewpoint and Capability Viewpoint can be exemplified as services are procured and fielded to support the operations and capabilities of organizations. The structural and behavioral models in the OVs and SvcVs allow architects and stakeholders to quickly ascertain which functions are carried out by humans and which by Services for each alternative specification and so carry out trade analysis based on risk, cost, reliability, etc.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

984167068

**Related Pages:**

These resources support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information. The relationship between architectural data elements across the Services Viewpoint to the Operational Viewpoint and Capability Viewpoint can be exemplified as services are procured and fielded to support the operations and capabilities of organizations. The structural and behavioral models in the OVs and SvcVs allow architects and stakeholders to quickly ascertain which functions are carried out by humans and which by Services for each alternative specification and so carry out trade analysis based on risk, cost, reliability, etc.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c8ec0ed-c3d8-4b2f-a1f8-06546c9fdd3d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">984167068</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="271fc43a-055c-4abe-84bd-35e55909a854" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990899 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00558: Setting up Automatic Population of Measurement Sets

- page_id: `153990899`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990899/Setting+up+Automatic+Population+of+Measurement+Sets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-16T11:06:56.889+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > Procedures > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['set-up-automatic-population-of-measurement-sets']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can set up the automatic measurementSet population each time creating the Instance of the associated type. 
 
 To set up the automatic Measurement Set population

1. Create a Measurement Set.
2. Add measurements for the created Measurement Set.
3. Apply « DoDAF Properties» stereotype to the created Measurement Set.
4. Open the Measurement Set Specification window and make sure the Expert properties mode is selected.
5. In the Tags property group, select the appliesFor and click the Create Value button.
6. Select the Exchange Element stereotype as the appliesFor tag value. [ATTACHMENT filename='exchange_element_as_the appliedFor_tag_value.png']
7. Save and reload the project.

You can see and edit your custom measurements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you want to see your measurements in a separate group within Exchange Element specification, add group name to the Measurement Set documentation field.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990752 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00562: Standard

- page_id: `153990752`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990752/Standard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:33:55.155+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Standards Viewpoint and Technical Standards Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

555942772

555942774

555942773

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: A ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture via the [constrainedItem] property of UML::Constraint.

DoDAF: A formal agreement documenting generally accepted specifications or criteria for products, processes, procedures, policies, systems, and/or personnel.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Properties

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| InformationTechnologyStandardCategory: String[*] | The information technology standard category which the «Standard» belongs to. |
| currentStatus : String[1] | The current status of the Standard. |
| mandatedDate : ISO8601DateTime[1] | The date the version of the standard was published. |
| ratifiedBy : ActualOrganization[1] | The organization that ratified this Standard. |
| retiredDate : ISO8601DateTime[1] | The date the version of the standard was retired. |
| shortName : String[1] | The short name of the standard. |
| version : String[1] | Represents the revision number of the standard, e.g., “1.2.1”, “v2”, “:2004”, etc. |

##### Extensions

UML Class

##### Related DoDAF views

[StdV-1 Standards Profile](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/StdV-1+Standards+Profile)

[StdV-2 Standards Forecast](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/StdV-2+Standards+Forecast)

[SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/SV-2+Systems+Internal+Resource+Flow+Description)

[SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/SV-9+Systems+Technology+and+Skills+Forecast)

##### Related MODAF views

[MODAF. SV-2 Resource Internal Communications Description](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+SV-2+Resource+Internal+Communications+Description)

[MODAF. SV-9 Technology and Skills Forecast](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+SV-9+Technology+and+Skills+Forecast)

[MODAF. TV-1 Standards Profile](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP190/MODAF.+TV-1+Standards+Profile)

[MODAF. TV-2 Standards Forecast](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P2024x/MODAF.+TV-2+Standards+Forecast)

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

916761723

916761725

916761724

##### Description

MODAF/NAF: An OperationalActivity that is a standard procedure that is doctrinal.

This is equivalent to what some defense organizations call JETLs.

DoDAF: Work, not specific to a single organization, weapon system or individual, that transforms inputs into outputs or changes their state (DoDAF::Activity).

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Activity

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990166 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00565: Standards viewpoint

- page_id: `153990166`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990166/Standards+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-17T11:57:55.935+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views
- labels: ['standards-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Standards Viewpoint is the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of parts or elements of the Architectural Description. These sets of rules can be captured at the enterprise level and applied to each solution, while each solution’s architectural description depicts only those rules pertinent to architecture described. Its purpose is to ensure that a solution satisfies a specified set of operational or capability requirements. The Standards Models capture the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based, common building blocks are established, and solutions are developed. It includes a collection of the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria that can be organized into profiles that govern solution elements for a given architecture. Current DoD guidance requires the Technical Standards portions of models be produced from DISR to determine the minimum set of standards and guidelines for the acquisition of all DoD systems that produce, use, or exchange information.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

#### PANEL: Views

Views

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

These sets of rules can be captured at the enterprise level and applied to each solution, while each solution’s architectural description depicts only those rules pertinent to architecture described. Its purpose is to ensure that a solution satisfies a specified set of operational or capability requirements. The Standards Models capture the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based, common building blocks are established, and solutions are developed. It includes a collection of the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria that can be organized into profiles that govern solution elements for a given architecture. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990167 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00567: StdV-1 Standards Profile

- page_id: `153990167`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990167/StdV-1+Standards+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-11T08:25:24.772+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Standards viewpoint
- labels: ['stdv-1-standards-profile', 'stdv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The StdV-1 defines the technical, operational, and business standards, guidance, and policy applicable to the architecture being described. As well as identifying applicable technical standards, the DoDAF V2.0 StdV-1 also documents the policies and standards that apply to the operational or business context. The DISR is an architecture resource for technical standards that can be used in the generation of the StdV-1 and StdV-2 Standards Forecast.

In most cases, building a Standards Profile consists of identifying and listing the applicable portions of existing and emerging documentation. A StdV-1 should identify both existing guidelines, as well as any areas lacking guidance. As with other models, each profile is assigned a specific timescale (e.g., “As-Is”, “To-Be”, or transitional). Linking the profile to a defined timescale enables the profile to consider both emerging technologies and any current technical standards that are expected to be updated or become obsolete. If more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to an architecture, then a StdV-2 Standards Forecast should be completed as well as a StdV-1.

The intended usage of the StdV-1 includes:

- Application of standards (informing project strategy).
- Standards compliance.

**Implementation**

StdV-1 can be represented by a StdV-1 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Standards cited are referenced as relationships to the systems, services, system functions, service functions, system data, service data, hardware/software items or communication protocols, where applicable, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='UPDM2P2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space='UPDM2P2024x'].

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Functional Standard
- Technical Standard
- Protocol

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

As well as identifying applicable technical standards, the DoDAF V2.0 StdV-1 also documents the policies and standards that apply to the operational or business context. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990170 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00568: StdV-2 Standards Forecast

- page_id: `153990170`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990170/StdV-2+Standards+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T13:00:10.875+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Standards viewpoint
- labels: ['stdv-2-standards-forecast', 'stdv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The StdV-2 contains expected changes in technology related standards, operational standards, or business standards and conventions, which are documented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='UPDM2P2024x'] model. The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards need to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast' space='UPDM2P2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast' space='UPDM2P2024x'] models.

A StdV-2 is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems, operational, and business activities covered by the Architectural Description. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. A StdV-2 complements and expands on the StdV-1Standards Profile model and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture.

One of the prime purposes of this model is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the architecture and its constituent elements.

The intended usage of the StdV-2 includes:

- Forecasting future changes in standards (informing project strategy).

**Implementation**

The StdV-2 can be represented using a StdV-2 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

StdV-2 delineates the standards that potentially impact the relevant system and service elements (from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='UPDM2P2024x']) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast' space='UPDM2P2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast' space='UPDM2P2024x'].

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Functional Standard
- Technical Standard
- Protocol
- Forecast

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990623 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00569: Structural Part

- page_id: `153990623`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990623/Structural+Part
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:19:04.346+01:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > UPDM 2.1 Elements > UPDM 2.1 Capability Viewpoint and Strategic Viewpoint Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

917035894

917035897

917035896

##### Description

UPDM: An EnterprisePhase can be sub-divided into structural and temporal parts. StructuralPart describes the EnterprisePhase elements that describe the structure.

MODAF/NAF: Asserts that one EnterprisePhase is a spatial part of another, (MODAF::EnterpriseStructure).

This is a topological structuring relationship, hence the EnterprisePhase may be physically disjoint.

##### Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF

##### Extensions

UML Property

##### Related MODAF Views

[MODAF. StV-1 Enterprise Vision](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/MODAF.+StV-1+Enterprise+Vision)

##### Related NAF Views

[NCV-1 Capability Vision](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UPDM2P190/NCV-1+Capability+Vision)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990101 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00571: SV-1 Systems Interface Description

- page_id: `153990101`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990101/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T11:44:42.414+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-1-systems-interface-description', 'sv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-1 addresses the composition and interaction of Systems. For DoDAF V2.0, the SV-1 incorporates the human elements as types of Performers - Organizations and Personnel Types.

The SV-1 links together the operational and systems architecture models by depicting how Resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description. A SV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description (i.e., in a “To-Be” architecture), and so there may be many alternative SV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an “As-Is” architecture, the OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SV-1 to allow communication of key Resource Flows to non-technical stakeholders.

A System Resource Flow is a simplified representation of a pathway or network pattern, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e., a line with possible amplifying information). The SV-1 depicts all System Resource Flows between Systems that are of interest. Note that Resource Flows between Systems may be further specified in detail in SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description and SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix.

Sub-System assemblies may be identified in SV-1 to any level (i.e., depth) of decomposition the architect sees fit. SV-1 may also identify the Physical Assets (e.g., Platforms) at which Resources are deployed, and optionally overlay Operational Activities and Locations that utilize those Resources. In many cases, an operational activity and locations depicted in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description model may well be the logical representation of the resource that is shown in SV-1.

The intended usage of the SV-1 includes:

- Definition of System concepts.
- Definition of System options.
- System Resource Flow requirements capture.
- Capability integration planning.
- System integration management.
- Operational planning (capability and performer definition).

The SV-1 is used in two complementary ways:

- Describe the Resource Flows exchanged between resources in the architecture.
- Describe a solution, or solution option, in terms of the components of capability and their physical integration on platforms and other facilities.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with performers originally specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one performer to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']. Resource Flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024x']).

The System Functions performed by the Performers are specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P2024x'] specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-1 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Interaction in SV-1 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying military symbols' space='UPDM2P2024x']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Resource Port
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Capability
- Capability Of Performer
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Geo Political Extent Type
- Performer
- Fielded Capability
- Service
- Request

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SV-1 addresses the composition and interaction of Systems. For DoDAF V2.0, the SV-1 incorporates the human elements as types of Performers - Organizations and Personnel Types.</p><p>The SV-1 links together the operational and systems architecture models by depicting how Resources are structured and interact to realize the logical architecture specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description. A SV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description (i.e., in a “To-Be” architecture), and so there may be many alternative SV models that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an “As-Is” architecture, the OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SV-1 to allow communication of key Resource Flows to non-technical stakeholders.</p><p>A System Resource Flow is a simplified representation of a pathway or network pattern, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e., a line with possible amplifying information). The SV-1 depicts all System Resource Flows between Systems that are of interest. Note that Resource Flows between Systems may be further specified in detail in SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description and SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix.</p><p>Sub-System assemblies may be identified in SV-1 to any level (i.e., depth) of decomposition the architect sees fit. SV-1 may also identify the Physical Assets (e.g., Platforms) at which Resources are deployed, and optionally overlay Operational Activities and Locations that utilize those Resources. In many cases, an operational activity and locations depicted in an OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description model may well be the logical representation of the resource that is shown in SV-1.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-1 includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of System concepts.</li><li>Definition of System options.</li><li>System Resource Flow requirements capture.</li><li>Capability integration planning.</li><li>System integration management.</li><li>Operational planning (capability and performer definition).</li></ul><p>The SV-1 is used in two complementary ways:</p><ul><li>Describe the Resource Flows exchanged between resources in the architecture.</li><li>Describe a solution, or solution option, in terms of the components of capability and their physical integration on platforms and other facilities.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>A UML Class diagram.</li><li>A UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>A SysML Block Definition diagram.</li><li>A SysML Internal Block diagram.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-1.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with performers originally specified in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.</p><p>An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one performer to another. Resource Flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</p><p>The System Functions performed by the Performers are specified in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4 System Functionality Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.</p><p>An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4 ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="35a55bc5-335b-4a81-af04-4509603aa5d3" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990105 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00572: SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description

- page_id: `153990105`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990105/SV-1+Systems+Interface+Internal+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T12:16:10.218+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-1-systems-interface-internal-description', 'sv-1-internal']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-1 addresses the internal composition and interaction of Systems.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with performers originally specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one performer to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']. Resource Flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024x']).

The System Functions performed by the Performers are specified in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'], but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P2024x'] specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Resource Connector
- Resource Port
- Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SV-1 addresses the internal composition and interaction of Systems.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>A SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>A UML Class diagram.</li><li>A UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>A SysML Block Definition diagram.</li><li>A SysML Internal Block diagram.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:alt="SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description" ac:title="SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-1_internal_MODAF.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with performers originally specified in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.</p><p>An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one performer to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Resource Flows are summarized in a Systems-Systems Matrix (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</p><p>The System Functions performed by the Performers are specified in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4 System Functionality Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, but may optionally be overlaid in the SV-1.</p><p>An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="ab448c0e-0d13-452f-880d-a58298aebedd" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="9df9dbc6-a754-448f-98e6-f789ce45c97c" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="0ed8d3a8-8324-4a24-a156-9c412f7b2a77" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Role</li><li>Resource Interface</li><li>Resource Connector</li><li>Resource Port</li><li>Property</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990157 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00573: SV-10a Systems Rules Model

- page_id: `153990157`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990157/SV-10a+Systems+Rules+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-11T07:57:11.804+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-10a-systems-rules-model', 'sv-10a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-10a specifies functional and nonfunctional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (i.e., the structural and behavioral elements of the Systems Viewpoint).

The SV-10a DoDAF-described Model describes constraints on the resources, functions, data, and ports that make up the SV. The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e., non-functional).

The intended usage of the SV-10a includes:

- Definition of implementation logic.
- Identification of resource constraints.

Implementation

SV-10a can be represented using:

- A SV-10a table.
- A SV-10a spreadsheet report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In contrast to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space='UPDM2P2024x'], SV-10a focuses on physical and data constraints rather than business rules.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Constraint
- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Function
- Entity Item
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e., non-functional).</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-10a includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of implementation logic.</li><li>Identification of resource constraints.</li></ul><p>Implementation</p><p>SV-10a can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-10a table.</li><li>A SV-10a spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:alt="SV-10a Systems Rules Model" ac:title="SV-10a Systems Rules Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-10a_sys_rules_mod.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-10a Systems Rules Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In contrast to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-6a Operational Rules Model" /></ac:link>, SV-10a focuses on physical and data constraints rather than business rules.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Constraint</li><li>System</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization Type</li><li>Person Type</li><li>Function</li><li>Entity Item</li><li>Resource Interaction</li><li>Exchange Element</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990160 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00574: SV-10b Systems State Transition Description

- page_id: `153990160`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990160/SV-10b+Systems+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T12:55:21.565+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-10b-systems-state-transition-description', 'sv-10b']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-10b is a graphical method of describing a resource (or system function) response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. The SV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the functions. Alternatively, SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of system functions with respect to a specific resource.

The intended usage of the SV-10b includes:

- Definition of states, events and state transitions (behavioral modeling).
- Identification of constraints.

**Implementation**

SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of functions described in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. However, the relationship between the actions included in SV-10b and the functions in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] depends on the purposes of the architecture and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']. SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.

Depending upon the architecture project’s needs, the SV-10b may be used separately or in conjunction with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'].

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resources State Machine
- Resource State
- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the resources in the Activities respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>The explicit time sequencing of service functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. The SV-10b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the functions. Alternatively, SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of system functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-10b includes:</p><ul><li>Definition of states, events and state transitions (behavioral modeling).</li><li>Identification of constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="SV-10b Systems State Transition Description" ac:title="SV-10b Systems State Transition Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-10b_sys_state_transit_desc.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-10b Systems State Transition Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SV-10b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of functions described in SV-4 Systems Functionality Description. However, the relationship between the actions included in SV-10b and the functions in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link> depends on the purposes of the architecture and the level of abstraction used in the models. The explicit sequencing of functions in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. SV-10b can be used to reflect explicit sequencing of the functions, the sequencing of actions internal to a single function, or the sequencing of functions with respect to a specific resource.</p><p>Depending upon the architecture project’s needs, the SV-10b may be used separately or in conjunction with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="4faf469e-7ba5-4491-8de0-658c8bea2b51" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resources State Machine</li><li>Resource State</li><li>System</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization Type</li><li>Person Type</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990162 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00575: SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `153990162`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990162/SV-10c+Systems+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-11T08:11:40.814+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-10c-systems-event-trace-description', 'sv-10c']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-10c provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

The SV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or System Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.

The intended usage of the SV-10c includes:

- Analysis of resource events impacting operation.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identification of non-functional system requirements.

**Implementation**

SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-10b Systems State Transition Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SV-10c may be related with Resource Flows (the interactions in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024x']), Resource Flows (the data in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024x']) and entities (in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space='UPDM2P2024x']) modeled in other models.

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Event Trace
- Resource Message
- Resource Interaction

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SV-10c provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between functional resources. Each event-trace diagram should have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.</p><p>The SV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating resource or System Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, to perform its assigned functionality.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-10c includes:</p><ul><li>Analysis of resource events impacting operation.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identification of non-functional system requirements.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description" ac:title="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-10c_sys_event_trace_desc.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-10b Systems State Transition Description" /></ac:link> to describe the dynamic behavior of resources. The data content of messages that connect Resource Flows in a SV-10c may be related with Resource Flows (the interactions in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-3 Systems- Systems Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), Resource Flows (the data in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /></ac:link>) and entities (in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="DIV-3 Physical Data Model" /></ac:link>) modeled in other models.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Event Trace</li><li>Resource Message</li><li>Resource Interaction</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990165 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00576: SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description BPD

- page_id: `153990165`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990165/SV-10c+Systems+Event-Trace+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-06-08T13:32:43.580+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990112 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00577: SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `153990112`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990112/SV-2+Systems+Internal+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T12:23:29.988+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-2-systems-internal-resource-flow-description', 'sv-2-internal']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A SV-2 specifies the internal System Resource Flows between Systems and may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.

**Implementation**

SV-2 can be represented using:

- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UM L Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Any protocol referred to in a SV-2 diagram needs to be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='UPDM2P2024x'].

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Resource Connector
- Resource Port
- Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A SV-2 specifies the internal System Resource Flows between Systems and may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-2 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>A SV-2 <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990109 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00578: SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description

- page_id: `153990109`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990109/SV-2+Systems+Resource+Flow+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T12:23:50.294+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-2-systems-resource-flow-description', 'sv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A SV-2 specifies the System Resource Flows between Systems and may also list the protocol stacks used in connections.

A SV-2 DoDAF-described Model is used to give a precise specification of a connection between Systems. This may be an existing connection, or a specification for a connection that is to be made.

The intended usage of the SV-2 includes:

- Resource Flow specification.

**Implementation**

SV-2 can be represented using:

- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- A SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A UM L Class diagram.
- A UML Composite Structure diagram.
- A SysML Block Definition diagram.
- A SysML Internal Block diagram.

Any protocol referred to in a SV-2 diagram needs to be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='UPDM2P2024x'].

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-2 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Interaction in SV-2 diagram' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying military symbols' space='UPDM2P2024x']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Resource Port
- Resource Connector
- Resource Role
- Resource Interface
- Protocol
- Functional Standard
- Technical Standard
- Service
- Request
- Energy
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Geo Political Extent Type

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990116 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00579: SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix

- page_id: `153990116`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990116/SV-3+Systems-Systems+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T12:26:12.967+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-3-systems-systems-matrix', 'sv-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A SV-3 enables a quick overview of all the system resource interactions specified in one or more [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] models. The SV-3 provides a tabular summary of the system interactions specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] model for the Architectural Description. The matrix format supports a rapid assessment of potential commonalities and redundancies (or, if fault-tolerance is desired, the lack of redundancies).

The SV-3 can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of groups of system pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose.

The intended usage of the SV-3 includes:

- Summarizing system resource interactions.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.

**Implementation**

SV-3 can be represented using a SV-3 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The SV-3 matrix consists of as much rows and columns as there are Systems in the matrix data source.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] concentrates on System resources and their interactions, and these are summarized in a SV-3.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Geo Political Extent Type

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990126 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00580: SV-4 Functionality Description BPD

- page_id: `153990126`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990126/SV-4+Functionality+Description+BPD
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-07-12T10:22:41.718+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the businesswork. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed businessactivities.

SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).

Some diagram elements have both UPDM and BPMN stereotypes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is based on BPMN Process Diagram (BPD). It describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>work. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">business </span>activities.</p><p>SV-4 Functionality Description BPD (BPD) diagram is similar to the BPMN Process Diagram (BPD).</p><p>Some diagram elements have both UPDM and BPMN stereotypes.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990120 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00581: SV-4 Systems Functionality Description

- page_id: `153990120`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990120/SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T12:29:43.819+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-4-systems-functionality-description', 'sv-4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-4 addresses human and system functionality.

The primary purposes of SV-4 are to:

- Develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource.
- Ensure that the functional connectivity is complete (i.e., that a resource’s required inputs are all satisfied).
- Ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail.

The Systems Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the:

- Allocation of functions to resources.
- Flow of resources between functions.

The SV-4 is the Systems Viewpoint model counterpart to the OV-5b Activity Model of the Operational Viewpoint.

The intended usage of the SV-4 includes:

- Description of task workflow.
- Identification of functional system requirements.
- Functional decomposition of systems.
- Relate human and system functions.

**Implementation**

SV-4 can be represented using:

- A SV-4 diagram for System Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='SV-4_hierarchy.png']
- An SV-4 diagram for System Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='SV-4_flows.png']
- A UML Class diagram.
- A UML Activity diagram.
- A SysML Block diagram.
- A SysML Activity diagram.

The SV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] (in the same way that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P2024x']OV-5b Operational Activity Model is the behavioral counterpart to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description).

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying military symbols' space='UPDM2P2024x']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Function
- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Activity Performed By Performer
- Function Action
- Function Edge
- Resource Parameter
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Geo Political Extent Type

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:alt="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" ac:title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-4_hierarchy.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>An SV-4 diagram for System Function flows. Activity diagram.</li><li>A SysML Block diagram.</li><li>A SysML Activity diagram.</li></ul><p>The SV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x"
