<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990127 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00582: SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `153990127`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990127/SV-5a+Operational+Activity+to+Systems+Function+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T12:35:31.556+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-5a', 'sv-5a-operational-activity-to-systems-function-traceability-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-5a addresses the linkage between System Functions described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] and Operational Activities specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P2024x'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P2024x']. The SV-5a depicts the mapping of system functions and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them to operational activities. The SV-5a identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.

During requirements definition, the SV-5a plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.

The intended usage of the SV-5a includes:

- Tracing functional system requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

SV-5a can be represented using a SV-5a diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. System Functions are used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term System Function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing; both activities and functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Function is a question of what and how.

The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A System Function is specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, SV-5a is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the OV-5a with the physical specification of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']. System Functions can be carried out by Functional Resources (systems, performers executing activities, and performers).

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- Function
- Operational Activity
- Implements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SV-5a addresses the linkage between System Functions described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link> and Operational Activities specified in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The SV-5a depicts the mapping of system functions and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them to operational activities. The SV-5a identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.</p><p>During requirements definition, the SV-5a plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system function requirements to those associated with user requirements.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-5a includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing functional system requirements to user requirements.</li><li>Tracing solution options to requirements.</li><li>Identification of overlaps or gaps.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-5a can be represented using a SV-5a diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. System Functions are used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" ac:title="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-5a.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>DoDAF uses the term Operational Activity in the OVs and the term System Function in the SVs to refer to essentially the same kind of thing; both activities and functions are tasks that are performed, accept inputs, and develop outputs. The distinction between an Operational Activity and a Function is a question of what and how.</p><p>The Operational Activity is a specification of what is to be done, regardless of the mechanism used. A System Function is specifies how a resource carries it out. For this reason, SV-5a is a significant model, as it ties together the logical specification in the OV-5a with the physical specification of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link>. System Functions can be carried out by Functional Resources (systems, performers executing activities, and performers).</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="b313a171-6d8b-432f-8526-8f596fcaf278" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> <ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="fc54f58e-9d3c-4bdc-9ec7-19d95fe07179" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="09cbc6d9-6d42-4ff3-b68f-d38b3baa9c2b" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Function</li><li>Operational Activity</li><li>Implements</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990130 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00583: SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `153990130`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990130/SV-5b+Operational+Activity+to+Systems+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-14T12:38:29.940+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-5b-operational-activity-to-systems-traceability-matrix', 'sv-5b']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-5b addresses the linkage between described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] and Operational Activities specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P2024x'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UPDM2P2024x']. The SV-5b depicts the mapping of systems and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them to operational activities. The SV-5b identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.

During requirements definition, the SV-5b plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system requirements to those associated with user requirements.

The intended usage of the SV-5b includes:

- Tracing system requirements to user requirements.
- Tracing solution options to requirements.
- Identification of overlaps or gaps.

**Implementation**

SV-5b can be represented using a SV-5b diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Systems are used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-5b is generally presented as a matrix of the relationship between systems and activities and can be a summary of the Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024x']). The SV-5b can show requirements traceability with Operational Activities on one axis of a matrix, the System Functions on the other axis, and with an X, date, or phase in the intersecting cells, where appropriate.

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Function
- Operational Activity
- Activity Performed By Performer
- Implements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The SV-5b addresses the linkage between described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1 Systems Functionality Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and Operational Activities specified in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5a Operational Activity Decomposition Tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The SV-5b depicts the mapping of systems and, optionally, the capabilities and performers that provide them to operational activities. The SV-5b identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.</p><p>During requirements definition, the SV-5b plays a particularly important role in tracing the architectural elements associated with system requirements to those associated with user requirements.</p><p>The intended usage of the SV-5b includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing system requirements to user requirements.</li><li>Tracing solution options to requirements.</li><li>Identification of overlaps or gaps.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-5b can be represented using a SV-5b diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Systems are used as row elements and Operational Activities are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix" ac:title="SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-5b.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The SV-5b is generally presented as a matrix of the relationship between systems and activities and can be a summary of the Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-5a]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). The SV-5b can show requirements traceability with Operational Activities on one axis of a matrix, the System Functions on the other axis, and with an X, date, or phase in the intersecting cells, where appropriate.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="b6463060-a272-4066-8e99-21ae169e71ff" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="2fa31fbf-526a-4ca6-b632-047fb943c97c" ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="a95b3a5c-79c0-4716-99ab-9d21042dff51" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>System</li><li>Function</li><li>Operational Activity</li><li>Activity Performed By Performer</li><li>Implements</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990133 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00584: SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix

- page_id: `153990133`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990133/SV-6+Systems+Resource+Flow+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-11T07:19:17.531+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-6-systems-resource-flow-matrix', 'sv-6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-6 specifies the characteristics of the System Resource Flows exchanged between systems with emphasis on resources crossing the system boundary.

The SV-6 focuses on the specific aspects of the system Resource Flow and the system Resource Flow content in a tabular format.

The intended usage of the SV-6 includes:

- Detailed definition of Resource Flows.

**Implementation**

SV-6 can be represented using:

- A SV-6 table.
- An SV-6 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Resource Roles (resource usages).
- A SV-6 spreadsheet report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SV-6 is the physical equivalent of the logical [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024x'] table and provides detailed information on the system connections which implement the Resource Flow exchanges specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UPDM2P2024x'].

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-6 table' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Custom Measurements' space='UPDM2P2024x']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Resource Interaction
- Exchange Element
- Energy
- Geo Political Extent Type
- Function

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=153990136 space=UPDM2P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00585: SV-7 Systems Measures Matrix

- page_id: `153990136`
- space_key: `UPDM2P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UPDM2P2024x/pages/153990136/SV-7+Systems+Measures+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-06-16T14:12:25.848+02:00`
- ancestors: UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation > DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints and views > Systems viewpoint
- labels: ['sv-7-systems-measures-matrix', 'sv-7', 'sv-7-actual-measures-table', 'sv-7-typical-measures-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The SV-7 depicts the measures (metrics) of resources. The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in a SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the resources in the SV-1.

The intended usage of the SV-7 includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measures (metrics).
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

SV-7 can be represented using:

- A SV-7 typical measures table. [ATTACHMENT filename='SV-7_sys_typical_mea_matrix.png']
- A SV-7 actual measures table. [ATTACHMENT filename='SvcV-7_Actual.PNG']
- A SV-7 actual measures spreadsheet report.

The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] by depicting the characteristics of the Systems and other Resources shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UPDM2P2024x']. The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'] by depicting the characteristics of the Systems shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UPDM2P2024x'].

#### PANEL: Related procedures

Related procedures

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-7 Typical table' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SV-7 Actual table' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating SV-7 Actual table from SV-7 Typical table' space='UPDM2P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='UPDM2P2024x']

#### PANEL: Related elements

Related elements

- System
- Software
- Capability Configuration
- Organization Type
- Person Type
- Measure Type
- Measure
- Measurement
- Actual Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The Systems Measures Matrix expands on the information presented in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by depicting the characteristics of the Systems shown in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="e7c5c8ac-ab38-4bb0-bc00-a61e93a89c8e" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related procedures</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating SV-7 Typical table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating SV-7 Actual table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Generating SV-7 Actual table from SV-7 Typical table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024x" ri:content-title="Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="858bd31e-8dd0-441c-be24-54e3014c0b26" ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>System</li><li>Software</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Organization Type</li><li>Person Type</li><li>Measure Type</li><li>Measure</li><li>Measurement</li><li>Actual Property</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
